tickets.title: bootstrap archive 9 ファイル / dirs (onlook + penpot + design-canvas + cookiecutter_legacy / SaaS friction 除去)

acceptance_criteria.text: The repository shall not contain any of the 9 archived items at the project root or under frontend/src/: onlook/, penpot/, frontend/src/components/onlook/, frontend/src/components/design-canvas/, frontend/src/app/workspaces/[id]/design/, services/cookiecutter_legacy/, plus 3 corresponding lint guards in scripts/lint-mock.sh (ARCHIVE check / emoji exempt list / AGPL block list). The pytest module test_t_019_01_archive_invariants.py shall mechanically assert each is absent.

When `bash scripts/lint-mock.sh --archive` is invoked, the system shall exit 0 with 'OK: ARCHIVE 残留なし' and shall scan frontend/src + backend for the string 'onlook' (excluding allow-listed ARCHIVE verification test files) emitting a WARN with exact file:line if any source reference reappears, and shall complete within 5 seconds for the full repo.

While the project is operational, the system shall keep frontend/package.json free of onlook / @onlook/* / @penpot/* dependency keys (NOTE: ARCHIVE removes only the self-hosted Penpot Docker stack at penpot/; Penpot SaaS API client backend/services/penpot_client.py remains as a separate integration), shall keep backend/requirements.txt free of cookiecutter / penpot packages, and shall ensure backend/main.py imports no module from the 9 archived paths; the smoke route count baseline (>= 300 routes) shall hold without onlook routers re-appearing.

If any reference to onlook / penpot reappears in package.json, requirements.txt, .tsx/.ts/.py source under frontend/src or backend (outside the documented ARCHIVE verification test files), or if any of the 9 archived directories is recreated at repo root, scripts/lint-mock.sh and the pytest module shall both fail with an explicit assertion identifying the regression (silent skip 禁止 / CI mechanical reject).

tickets.title: modify 対象 GitHub Issue 化 (scanner script + 運用文書 / side-effect free)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide `scripts/scan-modify-targets.py` which scans the codebase for 4 categories of modify candidates (deprecated_deps / stale_routers / stale_services / archived_keyword) and outputs structured JSON to stdout or --out file. The scanner is read-only and does NOT create GitHub Issues directly; conversion to Issues is documented in `docs/modify-targets/README.md` via `gh` CLI.

When the scanner runs, it shall return a dict with keys `total` (int count), `by_category` (count per category), and `targets` (list of {category, file, line, snippet, reason}). Exit code 0 on success, 2 on invalid arguments.

While the scanner runs, the system shall NOT mutate repository state, shall NOT call any external API (no `gh` / `curl` / GitHub API), and shall NOT write to audit_logs DB. Output file is the only side effect (when --out is specified).

If --category is not in VALID_CATEGORIES, the system shall raise ValueError and exit with code 2. If --out path is unwritable, the system shall print error to stderr and exit with code 2. The scanner SHALL NOT contain hardcoded GitHub tokens or repo URLs (auth handled by external `gh` CLI in docs).

tickets.title: 既存実装インベントリ監査 (51 routers + 75 services + 13 migrations + 9 integrations + 3 sandbox / 281 files / T-S0-13b で UNDETERMINED 0 化) → existing-inventory.json
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: scripts/audit-existing-inventory.py shall scan backend/routers/*.py, backend/services/*.py, backend/integrations/*.py, backend/sandbox/*.py, and supabase/migrations/*.sql producing docs/audit/2026-05-10_v1/existing-inventory.json with top-level keys: summary (dict containing audit_id / regenerated_at / scope / counts_by_classification / orphan_tickets_count / undetermined_remaining), inventory (list of 281 entries each with file_path + ticket_ids + label + mapping_status), and orphan_tickets (list of removed/missing paths); the classification enum shall be exactly (REUSE / REFACTOR / NEW / ARCHIVE / UNDETERMINED) and each entry shall carry a 1-line rationale field (reason or match_method).

When scripts/audit-existing-inventory.py is invoked, the script shall cross-reference each disk file against tickets.json `existing_files` lists, classify by ticket label (REUSE/REFACTOR/NEW/ARCHIVE), and emit a deterministic JSON (key-sorted, UTF-8, newline-terminated) plus a markdown view (existing-inventory.md); orphan_tickets shall list every path that tickets.json references but is absent from disk (e.g., onlook/ / penpot/ archived via T-019-01) with reason='removed per T-019-01 ARCHIVE; intentional'.

While the audit runs, scripts/audit-existing-inventory.py shall NOT mutate any source file (read-only filesystem access only), shall NOT require external network access, and shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010 main path invariant); the supersedes chain in summary shall preserve the regeneration history (audit_id='T-S0-13b' supersedes 'T-S0-13 (2026-05-10)') so T-S0-13c AC integrity check can verify lineage.

Where browser_use_service.py / browser_use*.py / litellm_router.py are present in the repo, the audit shall annotate each with a Phase boundary marker (Phase 1.5 / Phase 2) via `phase_boundary` field so downstream T-BTSTRAP-04 migration knows whether to REFACTOR or ARCHIVE; the summary.phase_annotations_applied counter shall be > 0 when at least one phase-boundary file is detected.

If a router/service cannot be confidently classified, the audit shall record it as UNDETERMINED with the specific reason instead of silently defaulting to REUSE; the regenerated existing-inventory.json shall have summary.undetermined_remaining = 0 (post T-S0-13b cleanup) — any non-zero count shall fail the spec test; the audit shall NOT default mapping_status to REUSE without an exact path match (REUSE is reserved for tickets whose existing_files list contains the exact disk path).

tickets.title: UNDETERMINED 64 件 → 0 化 + Orphan 6 件 → 16 annotated (T-S0-13 監査 follow-up / supersedes lineage / triage_needed=1 / phase_annotations_applied=15)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: T-S0-13b shall regenerate docs/audit/2026-05-10_v1/existing-inventory.json such that summary.undetermined_remaining = 0 (down from 64) and summary.audit_id='T-S0-13b' supersedes 'T-S0-13 (2026-05-10)' preserving the audit lineage; the regenerated inventory shall classify all 281 disk files into counts_by_classification keyed by exactly (REUSE / REFACTOR / NEW) with NO UNDETERMINED entries; orphan_tickets shall expand from 6 to ~16 entries each carrying a phase_boundary annotation (ARCHIVED / Phase 1.5 / Phase 2 / directory_reference) and a non-empty reason.

When the regeneration runs, each disk file matched to an existing REFACTOR ticket by basename / feature shall have its exact path appended to that ticket's existing_files (deduplicated); each previously-UNDETERMINED router/service shall be re-classified by re-running scripts/audit-existing-inventory.py + ticket walk; the regenerated JSON shall be deterministic (UTF-8 no BOM, } terminated, top-level keys = {summary, inventory, orphan_tickets}) so git diff stays clean across reruns.

While the cleanup is running, scripts/regenerate_inventory.py shall NOT introduce duplicate entries to any ticket's existing_files (set semantics via list-dedup), shall NOT mutate source files under backend/ / frontend/ / supabase/, and shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010 main path invariant); the supersedes chain in summary shall remain a string referencing the predecessor audit_id (T-S0-13).

Where multiple tickets could plausibly own a file, the cleanup shall pick the highest-specificity ticket using priority REFACTOR > REUSE > NEW (more specific label wins) and document the choice via the entry's primary_ticket field plus ticket_ids array (ordered: primary first, secondaries follow); the multi-ticket case shall be visible in existing-inventory.md so reviewers can audit the choice.

If a file cannot be mapped with confidence after the rule walk, the cleanup shall mark it as `mapping_status='triage_needed'` with a 1-line reason (e.g. 'placeholder reference; BA review pending') instead of silently assigning to the closest ticket; summary.triage_needed_count shall match the count of such entries in inventory; the regenerated JSON shall NOT contain any entry whose label or mapping_status is the legacy 'UNDETERMINED' string (post-cleanup invariant).

tickets.title: tickets.json 全件 AC 整合検査 (title ↔ AC + テンプレ転用検出 / verbatim 重複 0 / title-AC mismatch report / 178 tickets walk)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: scripts/audit-ac-coherence.py shall enumerate all tickets in docs/task-decomposition/2026-05-09_v1/tickets.json (179 entries) and produce docs/audit/2026-05-10_v1/ac-coherence-report.md containing exactly 4 sections numbered 1-4: (1) Verbatim 重複 AC text, (2) title↔AC キーワード乖離, (3) AC < 3 件 / insufficient, (4) AC 不在 / review_needed; the report header shall list Total tickets count and per-section counts (Verbatim 重複 AC text 0 / 既修正 PREVIOUSLY_FIXED ≥ 1 / title↔AC 乖離 ≥ 0 / AC 不在 review_needed ≥ 0).

When the audit detects a title↔AC keyword mismatch (e.g., title contains 'DDL' / 'schema' but AC contains no schema/table/migration term), the script shall record it under section 2 with both title and AC excerpt (first 3 lines); when a verbatim AC text appears in 2 or more different tickets (post canonical-EARS normalization), it shall be recorded under section 1 as a template-leftover signal.

While the audit runs, scripts/audit-ac-coherence.py shall NOT modify tickets.json (read-only filesystem access only), shall NOT require external network access, and shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010 main path invariant); the script shall emit deterministic markdown so re-running produces byte-identical output for the same tickets.json input.

Where a ticket already had its AC corrected in a prior PR (e.g. T-019-01 / T-S0-13 / T-001-01 / T-001-02 / T-001-04 / T-S0-08 / T-S0-09), the report shall mark it with `[previously_fixed]` annotation under its section 2 entry so reviewers can focus on remaining mismatches; this set shall include at least all 26+ tickets resolved in PR #159-#185 (T-006-02 / T-M30-02 / T-019-03 / T-S0-05 / T-005b-03 / T-011-01 / T-S0-04 / T-S0-01 / T-006-04 / T-010b-04 / T-019-01 / T-011-02 / T-S0-08 / T-020-02 / T-011-04 / T-S0-09 / T-012-03 / T-009-02 / T-S0-06 / T-M29-01 / T-010d-03 / T-010d-04 / T-010c-06 / T-017-01 / T-018-03 / T-017-03 / T-S0-13 / T-S0-13b).

If the audit cannot determine a verdict for a ticket (missing title / empty acceptance_criteria), the script shall list it under section 4 as 'review_needed' with a 1-line reason instead of silently passing; the audit shall NOT mutate any source file (read-only invariant) and shall NOT default a ticket's verdict to 'pass' when title/AC parsing fails.

tickets.title: docker-compose.yml 全サービス (postgres + redis + backend + litellm[profile] + frontend / healthcheck + depends_on)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide docker-compose.yml at the repo root with services postgres (supabase/postgres image; pgvector/pg_trgm/pgsodium), redis (redis:7.4-alpine), backend (FastAPI on port 8001 from backend/Dockerfile), frontend (Next.js on port 3000 from frontend/Dockerfile), and litellm under the 'litellm' profile (Layer 2b per ADR-010), connected via a single bridge network bf-net with named volumes postgres_data / redis_data / backend_data.

When `docker compose up -d` is invoked, the system shall start services in dependency order (postgres + redis → backend → frontend) using depends_on with service_healthy condition, and each of postgres/redis/backend/litellm shall declare a healthcheck (pg_isready / redis-cli ping / GET /api/health / GET /health/liveliness) that becomes healthy within 30s start_period.

While the docker compose project is running, the system shall expose configuration only via ${VAR:-default} interpolation from .env (no hardcoded secrets in docker-compose.yml), shall mount ./supabase/migrations into /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d for RLS bootstrap, and shall keep the litellm service opt-in via the 'litellm' profile so the main runner path (anthropic-python; ADR-010) is not coupled to LiteLLM at startup.

If docker-compose.yml hardcodes any secret (sk-ant-* / AIza* / JWT-pattern eyJ*), drops a service from the list, removes a healthcheck, removes the litellm profile (so litellm always starts), or references a deprecated image tag (postgres:latest / redis:latest), the pytest module test_t_s0_01_docker_compose.py shall fail with a specific assertion identifying the regression.

tickets.title: GitHub Actions ci.yml (pytest + coverage + pre-commit-check + smoke 統合 / 4 parallel jobs / actions @v4/@v5 pin / permissions:contents=read / concurrency cancel-in-progress)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The CI workflow `.github/workflows/ci.yml` shall run on every pull_request to main and every push to main, executing 4 parallel jobs (pre-commit-check / backend-pytest / backend-smoke / summary) and shall fail the build (exit_code != 0) if any job fails. ci.yml reuses existing scripts/pre-commit-check.sh without re-implementing the structural lint logic (REUSE 精神, T-S0-03 と同じパターン).

When the workflow runs, it shall execute (1) bash scripts/pre-commit-check.sh --quick (構造 lint + backend smoke), (2) python3 -m pytest tests/ --cov with deselect for known numpy-compat failure, (3) python3 -c 'from main import app' import smoke, and shall upload coverage.xml as artifact with 7-day retention.

While a pull_request is open, the workflow status shall be visible in the GitHub UI and shall preserve pytest baseline (>= 2823 passing tests excluding deselect). The workflow shall NOT modify repository state (permissions: contents=read) and shall use concurrency.group='ci-${{ github.ref }}' with cancel-in-progress=true so stale runs from earlier commits on the same branch are cancelled.

Where multiple commits are pushed to the same branch in rapid succession, concurrency.cancel-in-progress=true shall cancel the previous CI run for that ref so only the latest commit is fully tested (CI compute economy / faster PR feedback); all action references shall be pinned to specific major versions (actions/checkout@v4 / actions/setup-python@v5 / actions/upload-artifact@v4) — no @main / @master / @latest / floating refs (reproducibility invariant).

If pytest fails (excluding documented deselect), pre-commit-check fails, or main:app import fails, the workflow shall fail with exit_code != 0. continue-on-error: true shall NOT be used. The workflow shall NOT mutate persistent state (no writes, no audit_logs DB rows). The workflow shall NOT contain any hardcoded JWT (eyJ...) / sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-*; secrets shall always be referenced via ${{ secrets.NAME }}.

tickets.title: license-check.yml (AGPL 防御 + ADR-010 機械的ガード CI 実効化)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The CI workflow `.github/workflows/license-check.yml` shall run on every pull_request to main and every push to main, and shall fail the build (exit_code != 0) if AGPL/LGPL licensed dependencies are detected or if ADR-010 §UNWANTED mechanical guards (LangGraph/LangChain in main runner path, emoji baseline drift, tickets baseline drift, ARCHIVE residual) are violated. The workflow re-uses existing scripts/lint-mock.sh and scripts/validate-tickets.py without re-implementing detection logic.

When the workflow runs, it shall execute in sequence: scripts/lint-mock.sh --agpl, --no-langgraph, --emoji, --secrets, --archive (5 invocations) and python3 scripts/validate-tickets.py (1 invocation), and shall record the exit code of each step in the GitHub Actions log.

While a pull_request is open, the workflow status shall be visible in the GitHub UI and shall preserve existing lint script baselines (emoji=0 / tickets=0 / no AGPL deps / no ARCHIVE residual / no LangGraph imports). The workflow shall NOT modify repository state.

If an AGPL/LGPL dep is introduced into backend/requirements.txt or frontend/package.json, the workflow shall fail with exit_code != 0. If LangGraph or LangChain imports appear in the main runner code path, the workflow shall fail (ADR-010 §UNWANTED). If validate-tickets.py reports issues > 0, the workflow shall fail. The workflow shall NOT mutate persistent state (no writes to repo, no audit_logs DB rows).

tickets.title: deploy-staging.yml (Vercel + Oracle Cloud / Phase 1 ¥0 hosting / workflow_dispatch + tag trigger)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide .github/workflows/deploy-staging.yml that defines two jobs (deploy-frontend, deploy-backend) triggered on workflow_dispatch and push of tags matching staging-v*. The frontend job shall use vercel/vercel CLI to deploy frontend/ to Vercel; the backend job shall ssh into the Oracle Cloud Free Tier VM and docker compose pull/up the backend service from docker-compose.yml, then smoke-test GET /api/health.

When workflow_dispatch is invoked or a staging-v* tag is pushed, the system shall complete both deploy jobs within 30 minutes, emit a GitHub Actions summary line containing the deployed git SHA + target environment ('staging') + post-deploy smoke result, and shall fail the workflow if the post-deploy GET /api/health does not return 200 within 60 seconds.

While deploy-staging.yml is in use, the system shall reference secrets only via ${{ secrets.* }} (never hardcoded), shall target environment 'staging' (no production branch), and shall NOT modify .github/workflows/ci.yml or license-check.yml (independent additive workflow). The pytest module test_t_s0_04_deploy_staging.py shall mechanically assert these invariants.

If deploy-staging.yml contains hardcoded secrets (sk-ant-*, AIza*, JWT-pattern eyJ*), references a production branch (main without protection), targets pushes other than tag pattern staging-v* (no auto-deploy on every main push), or skips the post-deploy smoke step, the pytest module shall fail with a specific assertion message identifying the violation (silent skip 禁止), so CI shall mechanically reject the regression.

tickets.title: shadcn/ui setup + Tailwind config (verify existing setup / eb-500 + Lucide invariants)
tickets.label: REUSE

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall expose a pytest module backend/tests/test_t_s0_05_shadcn_tailwind.py that verifies the frontend shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4 setup: frontend/components.json shall declare iconLibrary='lucide' and tsx=true, frontend/postcss.config.mjs shall load @tailwindcss/postcss, frontend/src/app/globals.css shall import tailwindcss and define --color-eb-500: #1a6648, frontend/src/lib/utils.ts shall export cn(), and frontend/package.json shall list tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/postcss, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, lucide-react, shadcn.

When the verification pytest is invoked, the system shall complete all assertions in under 5 seconds and shall produce specific failure messages identifying which config file or token (eb-500 / Lucide / cn / required dep) is missing — no silent skips.

While the shadcn/Tailwind setup is in use, the system shall preserve CLAUDE.md §5.2 invariants: ENGINE BASE green eb-500 = '#1a6648' shall be present in globals.css and shall NOT be overridden by another value; iconLibrary in components.json shall remain 'lucide' (no @heroicons/@fontawesome/etc); the verification module shall not mutate any frontend file.

If components.json declares a non-Lucide iconLibrary, if globals.css drops the eb-500 #1a6648 token, if frontend/package.json adds a hard-disallowed icon library (@heroicons/react, @fortawesome/*, react-icons), or if shadcn/tailwind config files are deleted, the pytest module shall fail with an explicit assertion message (silent skip 禁止) so CI shall mechanically reject the regression.

tickets.title: 共通 UI components (Button/Input/Modal/Toast/Badge / shadcn + Radix UI + sonner / cva variant + ENGINE BASE green / Lucide-only)
tickets.label: REUSE

acceptance_criteria.text: frontend/src/components/ui/ shall expose the 5 canonical primitives required by CLAUDE.md §5.2: button.tsx (Button + buttonVariants cva), input.tsx (Input), dialog.tsx (Dialog + DialogTrigger + DialogContent + DialogHeader + DialogFooter + DialogTitle + DialogDescription + DialogClose + DialogPortal + DialogOverlay — 10 named exports; serves as the Modal primitive), sonner.tsx (Toaster + re-exported toast — serves as the Toast primitive), badge.tsx (Badge + badgeVariants cva). Each shall use class-variance-authority (cva) for variants when applicable and shall NOT redefine the design tokens (eb-500 / eb-700 are owned by tailwind.config + design-tokens.md).

When a consumer imports Button / Input / Badge / Dialog / Toaster / toast from @/components/ui/, the symbol shall resolve to the named export defined in the corresponding file; Dialog.tsx shall depend on @radix-ui/react-dialog for accessibility, button.tsx and badge.tsx shall depend on class-variance-authority for variant prop type-safety, and sonner.tsx shall re-export the toast function from 'sonner' so callers do not import 'sonner' directly.

While the components are in the tree, the system shall ship them WITHOUT emoji characters (CLAUDE.md §5.1 Lucide Icons only), without dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage, without direct backend fetch / axios / useQuery / useSWR calls (these are presentation primitives), and without hard-coded color hex values outside the existing eb-500 / eb-700 Tailwind class system (no #1a6648 literal in component files).

If a consumer attempts to instantiate Button with an unknown variant string at the TypeScript level it shall fail type-checking because buttonVariants is typed via VariantProps; if the Dialog primitive is rendered without a DialogContent child the Radix UI runtime shall throw (a11y contract); the component files shall NOT import 'reactflow' (legacy package, ADR-010 / T-009-02 invariant) and shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm anywhere.

tickets.title: Supabase FE wrapper (singleton browser client + auth helpers + graceful degradation)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The frontend shall provide `frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts` exposing `getSupabaseClient() / getCurrentUser() / signOut() / isSupabaseConfigured() / SupabaseNotConfiguredError / SupabaseConfigError`. The wrapper shall coexist with existing `api.ts` (backend REST) without modifying it (REUSE). Supabase usage is opt-in for code paths that need Auth / Realtime / RLS-aware direct access.

When `getSupabaseClient()` is called, the system shall read NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY env vars, validate URL pattern (https://*.supabase.co), and return a singleton client (cached on first call). When `signOut()` is called, the session shall be cleared in browser storage.

While env vars are unset OR @supabase/supabase-js is not installed, the system shall return a mock client whose methods return { data: null, error: SupabaseNotConfiguredError } (no throw, build/tsc passes). While env vars are valid, the client shall persist sessions in localStorage (persistSession: true).

If NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL does not match `https://*.supabase.co/` pattern OR NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY length < 10, the system shall throw SupabaseConfigError. The module source SHALL NOT contain hardcoded URLs / anon keys / service keys (env-only). The wrapper SHALL NOT write to audit_logs DB (backend-only concern).

tickets.title: claude-agent-sdk runner 基盤 (subprocess + session resume / SDK auto 3-tier compaction / 4-choice resume / ADR-010 main path)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/integrations/claude_agent_runner.py shall import claude_agent_sdk (Anthropic official) — explicitly NOT langgraph / langchain — to spawn Claude Code as a subprocess and capture stdout/stderr via the SDK's async message stream; the module shall expose ClaudeAgentRunner, SessionRecord, CostRecord, AuditEvent, SessionStore (+ DbSessionStore / InMemorySessionStore), CostHook (+ DbCostHook / NoopCostHook), SummaryHook (+ DbSummaryHook / NoopSummaryHook), AuditHook (+ DbAuditHook / NoopAuditHook), and VALID_RESUME_CHOICES.

ClaudeAgentRunner.run() shall accept an sdk_session_id parameter (Optional[str], default None) and pass it via the SDK's resume mechanism so the SDK auto-restores conversation history and applies 3-tier compaction; the runner shall NOT re-implement the SDK's auto-compaction (ADR-010 §自前実装禁止).

When ClaudeAgentRunner.run_task() is invoked, the system shall create a SessionRecord with status='running' via SessionStore.create_session() within 2 seconds, then stream each message line to session_logs via SessionStore.append_log(); the SessionRecord.status field shall be constrained to one of (running, done, crashed, cancelled, paused) consistent with supabase/migrations/20260510000003_runner_session_tables.sql CHECK constraint.

While the runner is active, ClaudeAgentRunner.cache_ttl_seconds shall default to 300 (5min ephemeral) so the SDK applies prompt cache automatically; the system shall record cache hit rate / input_tokens / output_tokens to cost_logs via CostHook.record() (DbCostHook implementation INSERTs into cost_logs with cache_read_tokens / cache_write_tokens columns per migration schema).

While context usage exceeds 95%, the SDK shall auto-generate a 9-section structured summary which the system shall persist via SummaryHook.persist() to chat_messages.compressed_summary (JSONB column per migration); the runner shall NOT generate the summary itself (SDK auto-feature reuse, ADR-010).

When the subprocess crashes (exception thrown by the SDK message loop), the system shall set SessionRecord.status='crashed' with crash_reason set, and ClaudeAgentRunner.handle_resume(choice) shall validate choice against VALID_RESUME_CHOICES = ('from_checkpoint', 'rerun_full', 'manual_fix', 'cancel') raising ValueError on invalid input; supabase migration sessions.resume_choice CHECK constraint shall mirror the same 4 values.

If `from langgraph` / `import langgraph` / `from langchain` / `import langchain` appears in backend/integrations/claude_agent_runner.py or any module on the main runner path, scripts/lint-mock.sh --no-langgraph shall exit non-zero (ADR-010 forbids them in main path); the pytest module test_t_s0_08_runner_invariants.py shall mechanically assert the import is absent.

tickets.title: OS-level sandbox 基盤 (bwrap on Linux + --unshare-net zero-trust / sandbox-exec on macOS / sandbox.violation audit-log + crash_reason='sandbox_violation' / T-S0-08 runner 統合)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/sandbox/ shall expose SandboxConfig (frozen dataclass with allow_paths / read_only_paths / allow_hosts / timeout_sec / cwd / extra_env), SandboxResult, SandboxError, SandboxUnavailable, SandboxViolation, run_sandboxed(cmd, cfg), plus internal _build_bwrap_argv and _build_sandbox_exec_argv. claude-agent-sdk runner (backend/integrations/claude_agent_runner.py) shall route all subprocess execution through run_sandboxed and shall NOT call subprocess.Popen / subprocess.run directly on user-supplied commands (sandbox bypass 禁止).

When run_sandboxed is invoked on Linux, _build_bwrap_argv shall include `--ro-bind` for every entry in SandboxConfig.read_only_paths, `--bind` for every entry in allow_paths, and `--unshare-pid --unshare-ipc --unshare-uts` for namespace isolation; on macOS, _build_sandbox_exec_argv shall start with `(version 1) (deny default) (allow process-fork)` and emit `(allow file-write* (regex ...))` only for allow_paths.

While SandboxConfig.allow_hosts is empty, the system shall deny outbound network on Linux by appending `--unshare-net` to the bwrap argv and on macOS by appending `(deny network*)` to the sandbox-exec policy; SandboxConfig.network_enabled() shall return False iff allow_hosts is empty (zero-trust default).

Where SandboxConfig.allow_hosts is empty (default), run_sandboxed shall NOT enable network at all (zero-trust); only when the caller explicitly provides at least one host in allow_hosts shall the network namespace be preserved (subject to bwrap kernel support); test_sandbox.py shall include a regression test that asserts `--unshare-net` is in argv when allow_hosts is empty.

If a sandboxed subprocess exits with sandbox-violation signal (SIGSYS) / EPERM from seccomp / bwrap detected escape, _detect_violation(rc, stderr) shall return a violation reason string ('write_outside_allow_paths' / 'network_disabled' / 'seccomp_blocked' / etc.) and run_sandboxed shall raise SandboxViolation; the claude-agent-sdk runner shall catch it, set SessionRecord.status='crashed' with crash_reason='sandbox_violation', and emit an AuditEvent with action='sandbox.violation' to audit_logs (no silent drop).

tickets.title: RLS context helper (auth_middleware → PostgreSQL session に SET LOCAL request.jwt.claim.sub 伝播 / async context manager / no production bypass)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/services/rls_context.py shall expose public symbols set_request_user(conn, user_id, custom_permissions=None), reset_request_user(conn), with_request_user(conn, user_id, custom_permissions=None) (async context manager), DEV_BYPASS_USER_ID, MAX_USER_ID_LEN=200, RLSContextError; the helper shall use Postgres `SET LOCAL request.jwt.claim.sub = '<user_id>'` and `SET LOCAL request.jwt.claims = '<json>'` so RLS policies relying on auth.uid() / auth.jwt() can read the per-request user identity.

When with_request_user(conn, user_id) async context manager exits (either via success path or exception path), the helper shall invoke reset_request_user(conn) to clear the SET LOCAL variables so the connection can be safely returned to the pool; reset_request_user shall use `RESET request.jwt.claim.sub` and `RESET request.jwt.claims` (not SET to empty string).

While operating with BUILD_FACTORY_DEV_BYPASS_AUTH=1 (local dev), the helper shall use DEV_BYPASS_USER_ID = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' (matching auth_middleware DEV_USER.sub) as deterministic fallback; the helper shall NOT bypass RLS when BF_ENV='prod' (the bypass env var is only respected in non-prod, enforced via bf_env_guard.is_destructive_allowed()) and shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010).

Where the user record carries custom_permissions JSONB, the helper shall serialize it into the SET LOCAL request.jwt.claims JSON payload so RLS policies can read `auth.jwt() -> 'custom_permissions'`; the JSON payload shall be safely escaped (no single-quote SQL injection) by using parameterized SET LOCAL via conn.execute('SET LOCAL request.jwt.claims = $1', json_str) where the driver supports it, or proper escape function.

If user_id is empty/non-string/over MAX_USER_ID_LEN=200 chars, or contains single-quote / null-byte (SQL injection vector), set_request_user shall raise RLSContextError BEFORE issuing the SET LOCAL statement; if RESET fails inside the context manager exit path, the helper shall re-raise so the connection is NOT returned to the pool with stale state; the helper shall NOT have a `force_bypass=True` argument (backdoor 禁止).

tickets.title: Sentry 設定 (FE+BE / error reporting + perf tracing / graceful degradation)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The backend shall provide `backend/sentry_config.py` (sentry-sdk graceful import) and the frontend shall provide `frontend/src/lib/sentry.ts` (@sentry/nextjs dynamic import). Both shall expose `init / captureException / captureMessage / setUser / setTag` and shall coexist with structlog (T-S0-11) and audit_logs (memory_service) as 3 independent observability layers.

When init_sentry(dsn, environment, traces_sample_rate, release, send_default_pii) is called, the system shall validate parameters, set the global SDK state (idempotent), and log success/failure to stdlib logging. capture_exception / capture_message shall return event_id or None (no-op when not configured).

While sentry-sdk / @sentry/nextjs is NOT installed OR SENTRY_DSN env var is unset, the system shall fall back gracefully to no-op for all public APIs (no ImportError, no crash). send_default_pii shall default to False (no PII / cookie / IP forwarded). max_breadcrumbs shall be capped at 50.

If invalid traces_sample_rate (outside [0.0, 1.0]) or invalid environment (not in development/staging/production/ci/test) is passed, the system shall raise ValueError. The Sentry module shall NOT write to audit_logs DB (separated concern). The module source SHALL NOT contain hardcoded DSN / API keys (DSN comes from env only).

tickets.title: structlog + pino (backend + frontend 中央集約 logger / 構造化ログ)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The backend shall provide `backend/logging_config.py` (structlog setup with stdlib logging fallback) and the frontend shall provide `frontend/src/lib/logger.ts` (pino logger). Both shall expose `configure / get_logger / bind_context / clear_context` (backend) and `logger / withContext` (frontend). Existing `import logging` / `logging.getLogger` calls SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE backwards-compat).

When configure_structlog(level, json_output) is called, the system shall set both structlog and stdlib root logger to the requested level. When bind_context(**kwargs) is called, the system shall add the key-value pairs to contextvars so subsequent log emissions auto-merge the context (structlog only; stdlib fallback is no-op).

While structlog is installed, the system shall use JSON output in CI/PROD_LOGGING environments and console output in development; while structlog is NOT installed (minimal CI runtime), the system shall fall back gracefully to stdlib logging without raising ImportError. The logger module shall NOT write to audit_logs DB (audit_logs is a separate concern via memory_service.emit_event).

If structlog/pino import fails, the system shall log a warning via stdlib logging and continue with fallback (no crash). If an invalid log level is passed (not in DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR/CRITICAL), the system shall raise ValueError. The logger module shall NOT include secrets / authentication tokens / passwords in log output (caller responsibility; lint check enforced via secrets scanner).

tickets.title: Better Stack uptime monitor (pull /health + push heartbeat / graceful degradation)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The backend shall provide `backend/uptime_heartbeat.py` exposing `send_heartbeat() / is_configured() / get_heartbeat_url() / APSCHEDULER_INTEGRATION_GUIDE`. The existing `/health` endpoint (backend/main.py L248) shall be reused unmodified for the pull-mode (Better Stack → backend) check. The push-mode (backend → Better Stack heartbeat URL) shall be opt-in via BETTER_STACK_HEARTBEAT_URL env var.

When send_heartbeat() is invoked, the system shall (a) validate URL form (scheme https/http, non-empty netloc), (b) POST or GET to the URL via httpx within `timeout_sec` seconds (default 10), and (c) return True on 2xx/3xx, False on 4xx/5xx/network failure. The function shall NOT raise on network errors (silent degradation, log warning only).

While BETTER_STACK_HEARTBEAT_URL is unset, send_heartbeat() shall return False without any HTTP call (no-op). While set, the module shall NOT modify repository state or audit_logs DB (uptime monitor is a separate observability concern from audit trail). APScheduler integration shall be a guide (docstring), not auto-wired.

If url scheme is not https/http, or netloc is empty, or timeout_sec is outside (0, 60], or extra_payload is not a dict/None, the system shall raise ValueError. If network call fails (timeout / DNS / 5xx), the system shall log a warning and return False (NOT raise). The module source SHALL NOT contain hardcoded heartbeat URLs / API tokens (URL comes from env only).

tickets.title: Supabase project init + 環境変数 (env 必須化 / 4 vars / ハードコード鍵除去 / lint sb_*_key pattern reject / .env.example placeholder REPLACE_WITH_*)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/services/supabase_client.py shall declare _REQUIRED = ('SUPABASE_URL', 'SUPABASE_ANON_KEY', 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY', 'SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET') exactly (4 vars / no hardcoded fallbacks); .env.example shall list those 4 vars + SUPABASE_BUCKET=artifacts (default) using REPLACE_WITH_<NAME> placeholder syntax; the lint script scripts/lint-mock.sh shall include a check_secrets() step (#5) detecting sb_(publishable|secret)_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,} pattern.

When supabase_client is imported and any required SUPABASE_* env var is missing, the module shall raise RuntimeError listing every missing variable name (`Supabase 環境変数が未設定です: VAR1, VAR2, ...`) at import time (fail-fast, no lazy stub fallback); the check shall be implemented as `_missing = [k for k in _REQUIRED if not os.environ.get(k)]` so partial population is correctly detected.

While the application starts up without a populated .env, backend/main.py loading shall transitively trigger supabase_client import and fail fast with a list of ALL missing SUPABASE_* variables (not just the first one); the module shall NOT contain any hardcoded fallback url / key strings (no `or 'https://example.supabase.co'`, no `or 'sb_...'` patterns).

Where .env.example provides example values, every SUPABASE_* line shall use the REPLACE_WITH_<NAME> placeholder syntax (matched by regex `REPLACE_WITH_[A-Z_]+`) so the example file passes the secret-leak lint check; the file shall NOT contain any value that matches the production key pattern sb_(publishable|secret)_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}.

If a real-looking Supabase key (matching `^sb_(publishable|secret)_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}$`) is committed to any tracked file (excluding .env / .env.example which are gitignored or use placeholders), scripts/lint-mock.sh check_secrets() shall exit non-zero with the file:line hits; the production code shall NOT use `default=` arguments for these env reads to prevent silent fallback.

tickets.title: FastAPI モジュラーモノリス 13 ドメイン bounded-context 整理
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The backend code shall be organized into 13 bounded-context domain modules under backend/domains/.
Each domain module shall expose only its public interface to other modules via a domains/<name>/__init__.py barrel.
When a domain imports another domain's internal model directly (bypassing the barrel), the linter shall report a violation.
While building the application, the system shall verify the absence of circular dependencies between domain modules.
If circular dependencies are detected between domains, the build shall fail with the cycle path printed.

tickets.title: 認証 6 テーブル DDL + RLS (supabase/migrations/20260510000000_auth_tables.sql / users + auth_sessions + user_2fa_secrets + user_2fa_recovery_codes + oauth_connections + auth_audit_log / RLS auth.uid() owner)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: supabase/migrations/20260510000000_auth_tables.sql shall declare exactly 6 auth tables via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (users / auth_sessions / user_2fa_secrets / user_2fa_recovery_codes / oauth_connections / auth_audit_log) with explicit FK constraints referencing auth.users(id) and TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() for created_at columns; the file shall be idempotent (re-application produces no error).

When the migration is applied a second time, every CREATE statement shall use IF NOT EXISTS (tables) or DROP POLICY IF EXISTS + CREATE POLICY (RLS policies) so the migration is fully idempotent; no CREATE TABLE without IF NOT EXISTS shall appear for any of the 6 auth tables.

While RLS is enabled on auth_sessions / user_2fa_secrets / user_2fa_recovery_codes / oauth_connections, only the row's owner (auth.uid() = user_id) and service_role shall read or write rows; the migration shall declare ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY for each of those 4 tables and shall NOT grant blanket FOR ALL TO public access (public access禁止 invariant).

Where 2FA is enabled (user_2fa_secrets.enabled=true), the auth_audit_log shall record every TOTP challenge with success column (boolean) and user_id FK; the user_2fa_secrets table shall have an `enabled` BOOLEAN column with sensible default (DEFAULT false) so opt-in is explicit.

If a duplicate (user_id, provider) is inserted into oauth_connections, a UNIQUE constraint (user_id, provider) shall reject it at the DB level; if a 2FA secret is stored without encryption, the schema shall not allow it (encrypted_secret TEXT NOT NULL); the migration shall NOT contain any hardcoded JWT (eyJ...) / sb_publishable_* / sb_secret_* literal.

tickets.title: AI 5 テーブル DDL (hierarchy + clone / 20260512200000_ai_hierarchy_clone_tables.sql / ai_employees + ai_personas + ai_hierarchies + ai_clones + user_interaction_log)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: supabase/migrations/20260512200000_ai_hierarchy_clone_tables.sql shall declare exactly 5 AI-related tables via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (ai_employees / ai_personas / ai_hierarchies / ai_clones / user_interaction_log); the file shall be idempotent (re-application produces no error) and shall include explicit FK constraints linking ai_clones / ai_hierarchies back to ai_employees(id) (hierarchy graph integrity).

When the migration is applied a second time, every CREATE TABLE shall use IF NOT EXISTS and every CREATE INDEX shall use IF NOT EXISTS so the migration is fully idempotent; cycle prevention is enforced by the companion migration 20260512300000_cycle_prevention_triggers.sql via recursive CTE trigger on ai_hierarchies (no self-loop / no cycle insertion allowed).

While ai_clones is operational, the user_id column shall be NOT NULL UNIQUE (1 user = 1 clone) and a TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() created_at column shall be present on every of the 5 tables; the BMAD persona seed migration (20260512400000_bmad_personas_seed.sql) shall populate ai_personas with the 10 BMAD roles (mary / preston / winston / sally / devon / quinn / reviewer / brand / mockup / curator) consistent with CLAUDE.md §3 §AI 社員.

Where ai_hierarchies forms a tree, the schema shall allow nullable parent_id so root-level employees (with no manager) are representable; ai_clones shall have an `is_opted_in` BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE column so opt-in is explicit (per CLAUDE.md §3 user_clone_opt_in invariant).

If a duplicate user_id is inserted into ai_clones, the UNIQUE constraint on user_id shall reject it; if a self-loop (parent_id = child_id) is inserted into ai_hierarchies, the no_self_parent CHECK constraint shall reject it; if a deeper cycle is inserted, the recursive CTE trigger from companion migration 20260512300000_cycle_prevention_triggers.sql shall reject it; the migration shall NOT contain hardcoded JWT (eyJ...) / sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-*.

tickets.title: Build-Factory プロジェクト 11 テーブル DDL + RLS (supabase/migrations/20260510000001_bf_project_tables.sql / bf_projects + 10 child tables / workspace_id FK / EARS CHECK / self-loop CHECK)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: supabase/migrations/20260510000001_bf_project_tables.sql shall declare exactly 11 Build-Factory project tables via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (bf_projects / bf_phases / bf_features / bf_tasks / bf_task_dependencies / bf_acceptance_criteria / bf_constitutions / bf_constitution_revisions / bf_mocks / bf_deliveries / audit_logs) with workspace_id REFERENCES workspaces(id) FK on workspace-scoped tables and TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() created_at columns; the file shall be fully idempotent.

When the migration is applied a second time, every CREATE TABLE shall use IF NOT EXISTS and every CREATE INDEX shall use IF NOT EXISTS (re-application produces no error); CREATE POLICY shall be preceded by DROP POLICY IF EXISTS so the RLS layer is also idempotent.

While RLS is enabled on workspace-scoped tables (bf_projects / bf_phases / bf_features / bf_tasks / bf_task_dependencies / bf_acceptance_criteria / bf_constitutions / bf_constitution_revisions / bf_mocks / bf_deliveries), policies shall use auth.uid() / workspace_members membership check (joined via workspace_id) and service_role grant; the migration shall NOT contain any blanket FOR ALL TO public statement (public access禁止 invariant).

Where a bf_constitutions row exists, the principles JSONB column shall not be NULL (NOT NULL constraint) and bf_constitution_revisions shall reference bf_constitutions(id) so revision history is preserved across edits; the EARS enum CHECK on bf_acceptance_criteria.ears_type shall include all 5 forms (UBIQUITOUS, EVENT-DRIVEN or EVENT, STATE-DRIVEN or STATE, OPTIONAL, UNWANTED) so legacy alias migrations remain valid.

If bf_acceptance_criteria.ears_type is not one of (UBIQUITOUS / EVENT / STATE / OPTIONAL / UNWANTED), a CHECK constraint shall reject the row (current DDL uses 5-form enum with EVENT/STATE as canonical; the JSON-Schema validator in scripts/validate-ears-ac.py accepts both EVENT and EVENT-DRIVEN aliases at the application layer); if bf_task_dependencies inserts a self-loop (depends_on_task_id = task_id), the no_self_dep CHECK shall reject; the migration shall NOT contain any hardcoded sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-* / JWT (eyJ...) literal.

tickets.title: 実装・連携・運用 17 テーブル + Template DDL (chat_threads / chat_messages は T-S0-08 runner_session_tables.sql に同梱 / impl_integration_ops_tables.sql / red_lines + api_keys + slack_webhooks + github_repos + obsidian_vaults + templates 等)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: supabase/migrations/20260512000000_impl_integration_ops_tables.sql shall declare exactly 17 tables via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS (session_artifacts / prs / pr_reviews / red_lines / red_line_violations / llm_providers / api_keys / slack_webhooks / github_repos / obsidian_vaults / notifications / token_limits / backups / user_settings / workspace_settings / schema_versions / templates); the companion runner_session_tables migration shall additionally declare chat_threads + chat_messages tables (M-30 memory layer); all CREATE statements shall be idempotent.

When the migration is applied a second time, every CREATE TABLE shall use IF NOT EXISTS and every CREATE INDEX shall use IF NOT EXISTS so the migration is fully idempotent; the schema_versions table shall be populated by the migration with a row marking the schema version applied (audit trail for backward-compat).

While the integration tables are operational, api_keys shall reference encrypted_secrets via (secret_scope, secret_key) both NOT NULL (plaintext API key 禁止 / pgsodium 二重暗号化 invariant) and obsidian_vaults.vault_path shall be TEXT NOT NULL (per-user vault 必須); red_line_violations shall have red_line_id BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES red_lines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE so cascade delete is consistent; the migration shall NOT contain blanket FOR ALL TO public (public access禁止 invariant).

Where templates is populated, the templates.template_kind column shall have a CHECK constraint enumerating exactly 5 kinds (task / pr / mock / prompt / constitution) so application code can safely switch on the value; backups shall have a status CHECK constraint with 3 values (completed / failed / in_progress) and a backup_kind CHECK with 4 values (db / storage / obsidian / full).

If an api_key is inserted without (secret_scope, secret_key) (= encrypted_secrets reference), the NOT NULL constraints shall reject (raw plaintext value column 禁止); if a red_line_violation is inserted without a valid red_line_id FK, the FK constraint shall reject; the migration shall NOT contain any hardcoded sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-* / JWT (eyJ...) literal.

tickets.title: RLS 全 23 ユーザデータテーブル enforcement + custom_permissions 連動 (supabase/migrations/20260510000002_rls_full_enforcement.sql / 40 CREATE POLICY / bf_can_access_workspace helper / service_role bypass)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: supabase/migrations/20260510000002_rls_full_enforcement.sql shall enforce Row Level Security (RLS) on at least 20 user-data tables via ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY and shall define at least 30 RLS policies via CREATE POLICY; the migration shall include a workspace access helper (e.g. bf_can_access_workspace(ws_id) SQL function joining via workspace_members) used by all workspace-scoped policies.

When a user queries a row whose workspace_id is not in their workspace_members set, the RLS policy shall filter the row out (zero rows returned) — guaranteed by USING (bf_can_access_workspace(workspace_id)) clause on workspace-scoped tables; the migration shall use DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before each CREATE POLICY so re-application is idempotent.

While operating as service_role, the system shall bypass RLS via Supabase's built-in service_role privilege (no explicit FOR ALL TO service_role line is required because service_role inherently bypasses RLS); the migration shall NOT contain blanket FOR ALL TO public statements (public access禁止 invariant) and shall NOT disable RLS once enabled (no ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY in this file).

Where the user is an account_owner via account_members or workspace_members with role='owner', additional policies on accounts / workspaces tables shall grant cross-workspace read; the bf_can_access_workspace helper function shall be invoked by USING / WITH CHECK clauses to centralize the membership rule (DRY).

If RLS is disabled on any user-data table in this migration (ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY), the spec test shall fail; if a policy is missing DROP POLICY IF EXISTS counterpart (idempotency violation), the test shall warn; the migration shall NOT contain any hardcoded JWT (eyJ...) / sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-*.

tickets.title: 拡張機能 4 種 (vector + pg_trgm + pgsodium + pg_cron) + GIN/BRIN/partial index (20260501220100_pgvector.sql + 20260512100000_extensions_pgsodium_pgcron_indexes.sql / 全 migrations 計 150+ index)
tickets.label: REUSE

acceptance_criteria.text: supabase/migrations/20260501220100_pgvector.sql shall declare CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS for vector and pg_trgm; supabase/migrations/20260512100000_extensions_pgsodium_pgcron_indexes.sql shall declare CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS for pgsodium and pg_cron; across all migrations the combined CREATE INDEX (IF NOT EXISTS) count shall be >= 150 (covering B-tree, GIN, BRIN, and partial indexes).

When the migration is applied a second time, every CREATE EXTENSION shall use IF NOT EXISTS and every CREATE INDEX shall use IF NOT EXISTS so the migration is fully idempotent; the 20260512100000 migration shall include at least 3 GIN indexes (for JSONB columns like notifications.detail, bf_constitutions.principles, audit_logs.payload), at least 3 BRIN indexes (for time-series like audit_logs.created_at, cost_logs.occurred_at), and at least 3 partial indexes (e.g. WHERE is_active = TRUE).

While the extensions are operational, vector (pgvector) shall enable embedding storage (vector(N) type), pg_trgm shall enable trigram similarity search, pgsodium shall enable encrypted_secrets table-level encryption, and pg_cron shall enable scheduled tasks; the migration shall NOT contain blanket DROP EXTENSION statements and shall NOT downgrade extension versions (no extension version pinning that conflicts with Supabase managed defaults).

Where GIN / BRIN / partial index types are used, each CREATE INDEX statement shall use the appropriate USING clause (USING gin / USING brin) or WHERE clause (partial); GIN indexes shall target JSONB or text array columns; BRIN indexes shall target append-only timestamp columns for storage efficiency.

If any CREATE EXTENSION lacks IF NOT EXISTS (re-application breaks), or any CREATE INDEX lacks IF NOT EXISTS, the spec test shall fail; the migration shall NOT contain DROP EXTENSION (irreversibly removes extensions managed by Supabase) and shall NOT contain hardcoded JWT (eyJ...) / sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-* literals.

tickets.title: クローン opt-in trigger + service (bf_enforce_clone_opt_in / user_interaction_log BEFORE INSERT 拒否 / ai_clones.is_opted_in 確認 / GDPR opt-out grace period)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: supabase/migrations/20260512200000_ai_hierarchy_clone_tables.sql shall declare a PL/pgSQL function bf_enforce_clone_opt_in() and an idempotent BEFORE INSERT trigger trg_enforce_clone_opt_in on user_interaction_log table; the trigger shall raise EXCEPTION (errcode=check_violation) with message starting `clone_opt_in_required:` when the user has not opted in (ai_clones.is_opted_in IS NULL OR is_opted_in = FALSE).

When INSERT INTO user_interaction_log is attempted, the BEFORE INSERT trigger shall fire, SELECT is_opted_in FROM ai_clones WHERE user_id = NEW.user_id and raise EXCEPTION if NULL or FALSE; the migration shall use DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS before CREATE TRIGGER so re-application is idempotent.

While the trigger is operational, no user_interaction_log row shall be inserted for users with is_opted_in = FALSE (GDPR opt-in compliance / CLAUDE.md §11); the trigger function shall be LANGUAGE plpgsql and shall NOT bypass the check via SECURITY DEFINER (caller must be the actual user); backend/services/clone_opt_in.py shall provide a helper to manage opt-in/opt-out state via ai_clones table updates with audit logging.

Where a user has opted out (is_opted_in = FALSE after previous TRUE), past user_interaction_log rows shall remain in the DB until the GDPR 30-day grace period expires (handled by backend/services/clone_opt_in.py / scheduled cleanup); the opt_in_timestamp_consistent CHECK constraint on ai_clones shall ensure (is_opted_in = TRUE) → opted_in_at IS NOT NULL.

If a user_interaction_log INSERT is attempted without is_opted_in = TRUE, the trigger shall raise EXCEPTION with sqlstate check_violation; the migration shall NOT use SECURITY DEFINER on the trigger function (privilege escalation 防止); the migration shall NOT contain any hardcoded JWT (eyJ...) / sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-* literal.

tickets.title: 循環依存防止 trigger 2 種 (recursive CTE / bf_prevent_task_dep_cycle ON bf_task_dependencies + bf_prevent_ai_hierarchy_cycle ON ai_hierarchies / cycle_detected EXCEPTION)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: supabase/migrations/20260512300000_cycle_prevention_triggers.sql shall declare exactly 2 PL/pgSQL trigger functions: bf_prevent_task_dep_cycle() (attached as trg_prevent_task_dep_cycle ON bf_task_dependencies) and bf_prevent_ai_hierarchy_cycle() (attached as trg_prevent_ai_hierarchy_cycle ON ai_hierarchies); both functions shall use WITH RECURSIVE CTE to detect cycles and shall RAISE EXCEPTION with message starting `cycle_detected:` so callers can pattern-match the error.

When INSERT/UPDATE is attempted on bf_task_dependencies or ai_hierarchies and the new edge would form a cycle (recursive CTE finds a path from NEW.depends_on_task_id back to NEW.task_id, or from NEW.parent_id back to NEW.child_id), the BEFORE trigger shall RAISE EXCEPTION with errcode=check_violation; the migration shall use DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS before CREATE TRIGGER so re-application is idempotent.

While the triggers are operational, no cyclic edge shall persist in either table (acyclic invariant); the trigger functions shall be LANGUAGE plpgsql and shall NOT use SECURITY DEFINER (caller privilege enforced); the migration shall NOT contain blanket DROP TABLE / DROP TRIGGER without IF EXISTS guards (idempotency invariant).

Where a direct self-loop is attempted (task_id = depends_on_task_id, or parent_id = child_id), the trigger shall reject it without descending into the recursive CTE (early-exit via simple equality check); the recursive CTE depth shall be bounded (PostgreSQL default cte_max_recursion_depth) so pathological deep graphs do not exhaust memory.

If a cyclic dependency is inserted, the trigger shall RAISE EXCEPTION with errcode `check_violation`; if either trigger function lacks WITH RECURSIVE, the spec test shall fail; the migration shall NOT use SECURITY DEFINER on trigger functions; the migration shall NOT contain any hardcoded JWT (eyJ...) / sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-* literal.

tickets.title: 6 ロール enum + permission matrix JSON (account_owner→owner / workspace_admin→ws_admin normalize / 30 permission keys / role-oriented matrix / GET /api/workspaces/permissions/matrix)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall accept exactly 6 normalized roles {owner, ws_admin, contributor, viewer, client, monitor} as workspace_members.role values; backend/services/roles.py shall expose _ROLES_JSON_KEY_MAP (account_owner→owner / workspace_admin→ws_admin / contributor / viewer / client / monitor identity), get_role_definitions(), get_permission_matrix(), has_permission(), is_known_role(), validate_custom_permissions(), get_permissions_metadata(), get_role_oriented_matrix(); the canonical source is docs/functional-breakdown/2026-05-09_v1/roles.json (30 permission keys × 6 roles).

When GET /api/workspaces/permissions/matrix is invoked, the endpoint shall return a JSON object with shape {roles: string[6], permissions: [{key, label, category}][30], matrix: {role: {permission: bool}}, permission_keys: string[30], legacy_matrix: {permission: {role: bool|str}}} within 2 seconds; the matrix shall be deterministic (same response for same roles.json input).

While the application is running, _load_roles_json() shall cache the parsed roles.json via @lru_cache(maxsize=1) so disk I/O happens once per process; PERMISSION_MATRIX module-level constant shall be computed via get_permission_matrix() at import time; the roles.py shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010) and shall NOT redefine SECTION_KEYS / REVIEW_DIMENSIONS / PERSONA_NAME (G15 cross-module invariant).

Where a workspace member has a `custom_permissions` JSONB override on workspace_members.custom_permissions, has_permission() shall consult the override FIRST and grant True when custom_permissions[key]=True (v2.1 OR-evaluation: grant-only override on top of the 6 base roles, no revoke semantics by design); validate_custom_permissions() shall return a list of warning strings for unknown permission keys (silent fail-safe / log-only).

If a request supplies a role value outside the 6 normalized roles, the system shall reject the request with HTTP 422 and shall NOT persist the change; if roles.json file is missing or malformed, _load_roles_json() shall return {roles: [], permission_matrix: {}} (graceful empty) so the application boots even in test environments; the matrix endpoint shall NOT contain any hardcoded sb_(publishable|secret)_*key / sk-ant-* / JWT (eyJ...).

tickets.title: custom_permissions JSONB バリデータ (validate_custom_permissions / unknown key warn list / has_permission grant-only override / 30 permission key subset)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/services/roles.py shall expose validate_custom_permissions(custom_permissions: Optional[dict]) -> list[str] returning the list of unknown keys (subset of T-021-01 PERMISSIONS = 30 keys); the has_permission() helper shall consume the validated dict and apply per-key grant-only override (v2.1 OR-evaluation, consistent with T-021-01 AC-4 spec).

When a member is added or updated with custom_permissions JSONB, the layer above (workspace_members service / router) shall invoke validate_custom_permissions BEFORE persistence and shall reject the operation (HTTP 400) if the returned list is non-empty (unknown key detected); has_permission() called after the persistence step shall use the override value within 2 seconds.

While custom_permissions JSONB has key K with value True for a member, has_permission(roles, K) shall return True regardless of the role-default value for K (grant-only override); validate_custom_permissions shall NOT raise on None / empty dict (returns []) so the helper is safe to call unconditionally; the function shall be pure (no I/O / no DB) so it can be invoked from synchronous validation pipelines.

Where multiple roles are passed to has_permission (e.g. ['contributor', 'viewer'] for a user with composite role assignment), the OR-evaluation shall return True if ANY of the roles grants the permission OR if custom_permissions grants it; validate_custom_permissions warn list shall be deterministic (input-key-order preserved) so log output is stable across runs.

If custom_permissions contains an unknown key (not in PERMISSIONS = 30 keys), validate_custom_permissions shall include that key in the return list; if custom_permissions is not a dict (e.g. list, string, int), the helper shall handle it gracefully (return [] for None, but the upstream layer should reject non-dict before calling this function); the helper shall NOT mutate the input dict (functional purity invariant) and the module shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010).

tickets.title: Swarm 並列実行 (Subagent / Task tool + git worktree)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: Swarm shall spawn 4 / 9 / 16 / 64 parallel sessions, each via claude-agent-sdk Subagent (Task tool) + a dedicated git worktree + branch.
When the swarm starts, the system shall allocate one worktree per session under .worktrees/swarm_{pool_id}/cell_{n} to prevent file conflicts.
While a swarm is running, the system shall stream per-cell logs and aggregate stats (queued/running/done/failed/crashed).
Where two cells touch the same file, the system shall serialize them via a file-level lock and shall not allow both to commit simultaneously.
If a Subagent escapes its sandbox or attempts to access another cell's worktree, the system shall kill that cell and emit a redline event.

Where the matrix cell value is the string 'configurable' or starts with 'limited_', the renderCell() helper shall render a small amber pill (uppercase, underscores replaced with spaces) instead of a check / cross so reviewers can see context-aware permissions; where matrix data has not yet loaded, the cell shall fall back to the slate-300 'x' character (safe-by-default false rendering, consistent with services/roles.py safe-by-default invariant).

If backend returns HTTP 409 with code in {self_strip_blocked, owner_protected, unknown permission}, formatBackendError() shall render a user-friendly Japanese message instead of the raw error body; the module shall not import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010), shall not use dangerouslySetInnerHTML (XSS), and shall not contain hardcoded Supabase / Anthropic credentials.

tickets.title: self-strip block + owner 保護
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: If actor_user_id attempts to demote their own role from ws_admin to a lower role, the system shall respond HTTP 409 with detail={code: 'self_strip_blocked'}.
If a delete or role-change request would remove the last remaining owner of a workspace, the system shall respond HTTP 409 with detail={code: 'owner_protected'}.
When the actor changes another member's role (not their own), the system shall apply standard permission checks and shall not raise self_strip or owner_protected.

tickets.title: プロフィール編集 UI (frontend/src/app/settings/profile/page.tsx ProfileTab + AppearanceTab + Lucide User/Save/Loader2 + GET/PATCH /api/bf-profile + 5 TabKey navigation)

While profileQ.isLoading is true or draft is null, the page shall render a Loader2 spinner with '読み込み中…' instead of an empty form; while saveMut.isPending is true, the Save button shall be disabled and shall render a Loader2 spinner instead of the Save icon; the local draft state shall be detached from the React Query cache (separate useState) so that in-flight edits are not clobbered by background refetch.

Where the loaded profile has draft.display_name set, the avatar circle shall display the uppercase first character of display_name as the initial; where the user clicks 'リセット', the draft shall be reset to profileQ.data (revert all in-flight edits) so the user can abandon the form without reloading the page.

tickets.title: API キー管理 UI (ApiKeysTab + OAuthConnectionsCard + OAuthRow + PROVIDER_META slack/github/anthropic + workspace-api.fetchOAuthProviders / fetchOAuthStatus / startOAuthAuthorize / disconnectOAuth + sessionStorage oauth_state_${provider} CSRF guard + backend/routers/oauth.py callback expected_state vs received_state)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: frontend/src/app/settings/profile/page.tsx ApiKeysTab component shall render OAuthConnectionsCard + OAuthRow for the three OAuthProvider keys ['slack', 'github', 'anthropic'] with PROVIDER_META labels and lucide-react icons (MessageSquare / FileCode2 / Bot / KeyRound / Link2 / Unlink / Loader2); the row shall display a binary connection state derived from workspace-api.fetchOAuthStatus(provider, USER_ID) (GET /api/oauth/{provider}/status); ENGINE BASE green bg-eb-500 shall be used as the primary CTA color (no emoji per CLAUDE.md §5.1).

When the user clicks the connect button for a provider, the system shall call workspace-api.startOAuthAuthorize() (GET /api/oauth/{provider}/authorize?redirect_uri=...) and on success shall (a) store the returned state into sessionStorage under the key oauth_state_${provider} as a CSRF guard, (b) redirect via window.location.href = authorize_url; when the user clicks the disconnect button, the system shall call workspace-api.disconnectOAuth() (DELETE /api/oauth/{provider}?owner_id=...) and invalidate the React Query key ['oauth-status', provider, USER_ID] on success.

While statusQ.isLoading is true for an OAuthRow, a Loader2 spinner shall render in place of the connect / disconnect button; while providersQ.data is undefined, the row list shall fall back to Object.keys(PROVIDER_META) (defensive default to the 3 known providers); connection state shall be a one-way mirror of the backend (UI never marks 'connected' without statusQ.data.connected === true).

Where startOAuthAuthorize returns null (backend client_id missing / not configured), the UI shall alert the user with a JA message containing the provider label and '.env' hint and shall NOT navigate; where disconnect is requested, the UI shall confirm() with a JA prompt before issuing the DELETE so a misclick does not silently sever the connection.

If backend/routers/oauth.py callback receives expected_state and received_state that differ, the router shall raise HTTPException(400, {'code': 'csrf_mismatch'}) instead of exchanging the code (CSRF defense); the page module shall not import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010), shall not use dangerouslySetInnerHTML (XSS), and shall not contain hardcoded Supabase / Anthropic credentials.

tickets.title: pgsodium 暗号化保管
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: Every value persisted to encrypted_credentials.ciphertext shall be encrypted with pgsodium (or fernet-compatible fallback) and shall never be stored as plain text.
When get_credential(user_id, provider) is called, the system shall decrypt the stored ciphertext with the active key and return the plain text to the caller.
While multiple key versions exist (after rotation), the system shall use key_version to select the correct key for decryption of each row.
If no decryption key is configured at runtime, the system shall raise RuntimeError on get_credential and shall not silently return None or the ciphertext.

tickets.title: OAuth 連携 (Slack/GitHub/Anthropic)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall support OAuth integration for providers in {slack, github, anthropic} with authorize / callback / disconnect / status endpoints.
When the OAuth provider redirects back with a valid code, the system shall exchange it for an access_token and persist it via the credentials_store (encrypted).
When the provider returns an error in the callback, the system shall not persist any credential and shall display a user-friendly error to the frontend.
If the state parameter on the callback does not match the value issued by /authorize, the system shall reject the callback with HTTP 400 (csrf_mismatch).

tickets.title: クローン opt-in toggle + GDPR 削除権 (30 日 grace)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The user_lifecycle table shall persist clone_optin (bool) and deletion_requested_at (nullable timestamp) per user.
When a user submits a deletion request, the system shall set deletion_requested_at = now() and shall NOT hard-delete the user until 30 days later (grace period).
When a user cancels a pending deletion within the grace window, the system shall set deletion_requested_at = NULL and restore the account to active state.
If a user attempts to submit a deletion request while one is already pending, the system shall respond HTTP 409 with detail={code: 'already_pending'}.

tickets.title: LiteLLM docker-compose 追加 (ADR-010 Layer 2b サブ用途: cheap-batch / image / voice / fallback)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall add a `litellm` service to `docker-compose.yml` (port 4000, image `ghcr.io/berriai/litellm:main-stable`) and provide `monitoring/litellm-config.yaml` defining ADR-010 Layer 2b routes (cheap-batch=Gemini Flash / image-gen=Gemini Image+DALL-E / whisper / anthropic-fallback=GPT-4o / gemini-fallback). The service shall be opt-in via `profiles: [litellm]` (Phase 1 manual start). Main path (claude-agent-sdk + anthropic-python) shall NOT route through LiteLLM.

When `docker compose --profile litellm up -d litellm` is run, the litellm service shall start on port 4000 with healthcheck `/health/liveliness`, authenticated via LITELLM_MASTER_KEY env. The config shall include `num_retries: 2` and `request_timeout: 60` for graceful failure handling (cooperates with T-AI-06 tenacity).

While the litellm service runs, the system shall mount `monitoring/litellm-config.yaml` read-only and shall persist API keys only via env vars (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY). The service shall use json_logs:true (T-S0-11 structlog 整合) and shall redact user API key info in logs. PII / secrets shall NOT appear in service logs.

If LITELLM_MASTER_KEY env is missing, the service shall fail to start (no anonymous access). If hardcoded API keys appear in docker-compose.yml or litellm-config.yaml (literal sk-* / eyJ* / google-api JWT), the lint script shall fail. The service shall NOT be started by default (profiles requirement), preventing accidental usage in main path.

tickets.title: Memory 3 tier (claude-agent-sdk session + Memory API + Mem0 + Obsidian / unified facade / ADR-010 §自前実装禁止 + cross-module SECTION_KEYS 不変)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/services/memory_service.py shall expose a unified async API combining: Tier 1 (claude-agent-sdk session — raw messages via short_term_layer), Tier 2 (compressed + 9-section SECTION_KEYS summary auto-generated by SDK and persisted via persist_compaction() to chat_messages.compressed_summary), Tier 3 (Anthropic Memory API + Mem0 vector via mem0_bridge.py + Obsidian markdown via obsidian_sync.py). The module shall expose at minimum the public symbols: emit_event, persist_compaction, write_fact, merge_for_session, mirror_to_obsidian, fact_fingerprint.

When the SDK's auto compaction triggers at 95% context usage, memory_service.persist_compaction(session_id, summary) shall (1) write the 9-section summary to chat_messages.compressed_summary as JSONB and (2) emit a 'memory_compacted' event row to audit_logs with action='memory_compacted' within 2 seconds; the runner shall NOT generate the summary itself (ADR-010 §自前実装禁止 — SDK auto feature reuse).

When memory_service.merge_for_session(session_id, prior_session_id, user_id, ...) is invoked for a session referencing a prior session_id, the system shall merge SDK session_resume (Tier 1 via sdk_session_id) + Memory API recall (durable facts) + Mem0 vector top-k similarity search + Constitution (松本判断基準 system prompt prefix) into the returned system prompt; merge order shall be deterministic (constitution first, then memory_api, then mem0, then sdk_session).

While Anthropic Memory API is available (MEMORY_API_AVAILABLE / _memory_api_write returns True), memory_service.write_fact() shall write durable facts there as primary, then mirror to mem0_bridge.mirror_fact_to_mem0() as a vector copy for similarity search; SECTION_KEYS shall be defined exactly once in backend/services/mid_term_layer.py with 9 entries (context, goals, decisions, open_questions, actions, blockers, facts, preferences, next_steps) and shall NOT be redefined in memory_service.py (G15 cross-module invariant).

Where the user opts in to Obsidian sync (mirror_to_obsidian invoked), the system shall mirror Tier 3 facts to the Obsidian vault as Markdown files with deterministic fact_fingerprint(text) -> sha256-prefix file names so the same fact is never written twice; the mirror function shall return the absolute Path of the written file or None if disabled.

If Anthropic Memory API write fails (network / quota / auth), memory_service.write_fact() shall fall back to Mem0 only and emit a 'memory_degraded' event to audit_logs with the failure reason; it shall NOT silently drop facts (the function shall still return a success-shaped dict or raise an explicit MemoryServiceError so the caller can surface the degradation).

While the component renders, all colors shall use ENGINE BASE green palette (`border-eb-500` / `border-eb-400` / `border-eb-200` / `bg-eb-50`) per CLAUDE.md §5.2, and all icons shall be Lucide (Crown for secretary, Users for leader, User for member) — NO emojis (CLAUDE.md §5.1). The component shall NOT mutate input props or write to any backend store.

If `employees` is empty, null, or contains only inactive/invalid records, the component shall render a fallback message (`org-chart-empty` test-id) instead of crashing. If `role_level` is not in [secretary, leader, member], the node shall be excluded silently. The component SHALL NOT contain hardcoded color literals outside the eb-* palette.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide `data/personas/bmad/{persona_key}.md` for all 10 Build-Factory personas (mary/preston/winston/sally/devon/quinn/reviewer/brand/mockup/logan) following a 7-section format (Role/Personality/Tone Style/Catchphrase/Specialty/Constraints/Handoff). The 12→10 BMAD mapping shall be documented in `data/personas/bmad/README.md`. A loader `backend/services/bmad_persona_prompts.py` exposes `load_persona_prompt(persona_key)` and `list_personas()`. Existing `ai_employee_store` / `delegation_service` / `secretary_chat` SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE).

When `load_persona_prompt(persona_key)` is called, the system shall return the md file content within 100ms (1ms with cache hit) or None if file missing. `get_prompt_validation_status(persona_key)` shall return dict {persona_key, available, missing_sections, char_count} verifying 7 required sections are present.

While the loader is in use, the DB seed (`ai_personas` table from supabase migration 20260512400000) remains the cache layer; the md files are the source of truth. The module SHALL NOT write to audit_logs DB (read-only loader). Path traversal SHALL be prevented (resolve().relative_to base dir check).

If `persona_key` is not in VALID_PERSONA_KEYS (10 keys), empty, non-string, or contains characters outside `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`, the system shall raise ValueError (caller bug). If a md file is missing or read fails, `load_persona_prompt` shall log a warning and return None (graceful, no raise). The module SHALL NOT contain hardcoded secrets and SHALL NOT make external HTTP calls.

tickets.title: Intent 分類 (existing intent_preprocessor/mode_detector/skill_detector 統合)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: The intent_classifier service shall provide a unified read API (classify()) that runs the 3 existing detectors (intent_preprocessor.detect_explicit_intent / mode_detector.detect_mode / skill_detector.detect_skill) in parallel and returns a normalized IntentSignal dict with top_signal priority (explicit > skill > mode). The existing 3 detector modules shall NOT be modified (REFACTOR backwards-compat).

When classify() is invoked, the system shall execute the 3 detectors in parallel via asyncio, apply the top_signal priority rule, and emit an audit_logs entry with event_type='intent.classified' carrying top_signal_kind/value/mode/skill/has_explicit/latency_ms within 2 seconds.

While the classifier is active, the system shall preserve the existing 3 detector module symbol surfaces (cross-module assert) and shall maintain backwards compatibility (REFACTOR coverage >= baseline). Read endpoints (health) shall NOT emit audit events.

If the SDK / alternative LLM classifier backend (register_classifier_backend) returns invalid output (missing keys / invalid mode / wrong types), the system shall fall back to the 3-detector default path with a warning log (silent failure 防止). If invalid input or unauthorized actor is detected, the system shall reject with 4xx {detail:{code,message}} and shall NOT mutate persistent state. If application code re-implements intent classification in a way that bypasses the 3 detectors (e.g., inlined keyword matching outside the existing modules), the lint script shall flag it during code review.

tickets.title: Memory 統合 orchestrator (memory_pipeline; 3 層 short+mid+long → context 組立) + 統合テスト
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The memory_pipeline module shall integrate the 3-tier memory layers (short=raw chat_messages via chat_thread_store / mid=9-section compressed_summary via mid_term_layer / long=Mem0+Obsidian via long_term_layer) and assemble a unified context block for AI session prompts. Integration tests shall verify cross-layer invariants (9-section SECTION_KEYS / tier ordering / source attribution). The module shall NOT modify the underlying 3 layer modules (NEW + REUSE).

When build_context(thread_id, query) is invoked, the system shall fetch all 3 tiers in parallel via asyncio and return an assembled context dict within 2 seconds, emitting audit_logs entry 'memory.context_built' with tier_hit_count and total_chars.

While the orchestrator is active, the system shall NOT modify any of the underlying layer modules (chat_thread_store / mid_term_layer / long_term_layer) and shall preserve their existing API contracts. If any single tier fails to fetch, the system shall degrade gracefully (return partial context + log warning) rather than abort the whole pipeline.

If a tier fetch raises an exception, the system shall log the warning and continue with remaining tiers (graceful degradation). If invalid input (empty thread_id / oversized query / non-positive max_tokens) is provided, the system shall reject with 4xx {detail:{code,message}} and shall NOT mutate persistent state. If application code re-implements 3-tier compaction in the orchestrator instead of delegating to the layer modules, the lint script shall flag it during code review (ADR-010 cross-ref with T-M28-02/03/04 UNWANTED).
