tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide (a) `backend/schemas/ears_ac_schema.json` (JSON Schema Draft-07) defining the 5 EARS AC forms (UBIQUITOUS / EVENT-DRIVEN / STATE-DRIVEN / OPTIONAL / UNWANTED) with type/text validation and form-specific pattern (When/While/Where/If/shall), (b) `docs/templates/ears-ac-template.md` documenting the format for human reference, and (c) `scripts/validate-ears-ac.py` running jsonschema validation across all tickets. Existing `scripts/validate-tickets.py` and `scripts/audit-ac-coherence.py` SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE).

When `python3 scripts/validate-ears-ac.py` is run, the validator shall load the schema, validate Draft-07 conformance of the schema itself, iterate all tickets in `docs/task-decomposition/2026-05-09_v1/tickets.json`, and emit per-AC validation issues. Exit codes: 0 (all pass), 1 (1+ violations), 2 (schema or argument error). Supports `--task-id`, `--verbose`, `--strict`, `--schema-only` flags.

While validation runs, the script shall NOT mutate `tickets.json` or any other repository file (read-only). The script SHALL NOT call external services, write to audit_logs DB, or modify global state. Existing `validate-tickets.py` continues to run as a heuristic check in parallel.

If the JSON Schema file is missing or invalid Draft-07, the script shall exit with code 2 and a clear error message. If `--task-id` does not match any ticket, the script shall exit with code 1. The script SHALL NOT contain hardcoded API keys or external URLs.

tickets.title: EARS 形式分類 AI prompt + 書き直し suggest (rule-based + SDK backend hook)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide `backend/services/ears_classifier.py` with `classify(text, hint_type, use_backend)`, `suggest_rewrite(text, target_type)`, `validate_against_schema(text, target_type)`, and `register_classifier_backend(callable)` (G53 SDK hook). REST endpoints POST `/api/ears/classify` + POST `/api/ears/suggest` + GET `/api/ears/health` + GET `/api/ears/forms` are exposed. The AI system prompt `data/prompts/ears-classifier.md` documents the classification + rewrite contract. Existing `backend/schemas/ears_ac_schema.json` (T-025-01) SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE).

When `classify(text)` is called with a 10-2000 char free-form text, the system shall return a dict {classified_type, confidence (0.0-1.0), rewritten_text, rationale, warnings, backend_used} within 100ms (rule-based) or as fast as the registered AI backend allows. POST `/api/ears/classify` emits `ears.classified` audit event; POST `/api/ears/suggest` emits `ears.suggested`.

While no AI backend is registered, the system shall use rule-based classification (prefix keyword When/While/Where/If or shall) and template-based rewrite (REUSE T-025-01 schema patterns). While an AI backend is registered, the system shall validate its output against the 5-type contract and fall back to rule-based on invalid output (graceful).

If `text` is empty / non-string / < 10 chars / > 2000 chars, the system shall raise ValueError (caller bug). If `target_type` is not in [UBIQUITOUS, EVENT-DRIVEN, STATE-DRIVEN, OPTIONAL, UNWANTED], the system shall raise ValueError. If the registered AI backend returns an output not conforming to the 5-key contract, the system shall log a warning and fall back to rule-based (no crash). Hardcoded API keys SHALL NOT appear in the module source.

tickets.title: DAG 仮想化 + 階層折りたたみ (pure helpers + DagHierarchyControls / DependencyGraph 補完)

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide `frontend/src/components/dag/DagHierarchy.tsx` with pure helpers `computeDepths(tasks, edges) -> Map<id, depth>` and `filterVisibleTasks(tasks, edges, collapsedIds, maxDepth) -> Set<id>`, plus React components `DagHierarchyControls` (summary + maxDepth input) and `CollapseButton` (per-node toggle). The MAX_REASONABLE_DEPTH constant (50) caps cycle-induced infinite recursion. Existing DependencyGraph.tsx SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE: caller filters visible ids before passing to DependencyGraph).

When `tasks` / `edges` / `collapsedIds` / `maxDepth` props change, the controls component shall recompute visible count via React.useMemo. When the user types in the depth input, `onMaxDepthChange(parsedInt)` is invoked. When CollapseButton is clicked, `onToggleCollapse(id)` callback fires. Pure helpers are exported via `__testing__` for unit testing.

While the controls are rendered, the collapsed state is controlled (caller owns Set<number>) — the component does not maintain its own collapse state. Cycles in edges shall be handled gracefully (visiting set + MAX_REASONABLE_DEPTH cap, no infinite recursion). All colors shall be eb-* palette (border-eb-500/200, bg-eb-50, text-eb-500) and icons Lucide (Layers, ChevronRight, ChevronDown).

If `tasks` is null / undefined / non-array, helpers shall return empty Map/Set (no crash). If `maxDepth` is negative / NaN / Infinity, it shall be treated as -1 (no cap). If edges contain a cycle (A→B→A), computeDepths shall terminate within MAX_REASONABLE_DEPTH iterations. Hardcoded color literals outside eb-* palette SHALL NOT appear in source.

tickets.title: 依存追加/削除 drag&drop (pure validation + DependencyDnDPanel / cycle 防止)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall provide `frontend/src/components/dag/DependencyDnD.tsx` with pure helpers `validateNewDependency / proposeAddEdge / proposeRemoveEdge / applyPending` and React component `DependencyDnDPanel`. The validation enforces 5 rules: source/target must be integer > 0, no self-edge, no duplicate, no cycle creation (BFS check from target). Existing `DependencyGraph.tsx` SHALL NOT be modified (REUSE: caller wires React Flow onConnect/onEdgesDelete to proposeAddEdge/proposeRemoveEdge).

When the user drags an edge in React Flow, the caller invokes `proposeAddEdge(source, target, existing, pending)` which returns DependencyChange or {error: ValidationResult}. When the user clicks Undo on a pending change, `onUndo(changeIndex)` callback fires. When the user clicks Confirm, `onConfirm()` callback fires to commit pending to backend.

While pending changes exist, the panel shall display add/remove counts + per-change Undo buttons. The pending state is controlled (caller owns DependencyChange[]). All colors shall be eb-* palette (border-eb-500/400/200, bg-eb-500/50, text-eb-500) and icons Lucide (Plus, Trash2, Undo2, AlertCircle).

If source/target is not a positive integer (negative / 0 / NaN / Infinity / non-number), validateNewDependency returns {valid: false, code: 'dep.invalid_type' | 'dep.invalid_value'}. If source === target (self-edge), code='dep.self_edge'. If edge already exists, code='dep.duplicate'. If adding would create a cycle (BFS from target reaches source), code='dep.cycle'. Hardcoded color literals outside eb-* palette SHALL NOT appear in source.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall expose backend/sandbox/landlock_policy.py with public symbols LandlockPolicy (frozen dataclass: read_paths / write_paths / exec_paths / denied_syscalls / network_allowed), DEFAULT_RED_LINE_POLICY, DEFAULT_DENIED_SYSCALLS (=('socket','connect','accept','bind','listen','ptrace','reboot','init_module','delete_module','kexec_load','mount','umount','umount2','chroot','pivot_root')), detect_landlock_availability(), evaluate_command(cmd, policy, sandbox_cfg=None), audit_red_line(cmd, policy, sandbox_cfg=None), LandlockPolicyError; plus router backend/routers/sandbox_landlock.py mounted at POST /api/sandbox/landlock/evaluate, GET /api/sandbox/landlock/availability, GET /api/sandbox/landlock/default-policy. The module shall REUSE T-S0-09 SandboxConfig without modifying backend/sandbox/{config.py, exec.py, __init__.py} (REUSE invariant).

When evaluate_command(cmd, policy, sandbox_cfg) is invoked, the system shall return {allowed:bool, violations:list[dict{kind:str,detail:str}], landlock_available:bool, kernel_version:str} within 2 seconds; when audit_red_line is invoked and a red-line violation is detected (cmd writes outside policy.write_paths, references a denied syscall via known string pattern, or enables network without allow_hosts), the function shall return a deterministic list of violations sorted by kind ascending.

While the policy module is operational, the system shall keep DEFAULT_RED_LINE_POLICY frozen at import time (immutable dataclass / cannot mutate fields after construction), shall NOT redefine SECTION_KEYS / REVIEW_DIMENSIONS / PERSONA_NAME (G15 cross-module invariant), and detect_landlock_availability() shall be deterministic for a given kernel (cache result per-process).

If cmd is empty/non-list/over 200 elements, policy is non-LandlockPolicy, sandbox_cfg is non-SandboxConfig, or any policy path is non-absolute (relative paths break Landlock), the module shall raise LandlockPolicyError mapped to 4xx {detail:{code:'landlock_policy.invalid_input'|'landlock_policy.unauthorized', message}} and shall NOT mutate state on validation failure; the router shall return 4xx without touching the default policy singleton.

When plan_review or run_plan_gen_eval is invoked, the system shall return {plan|review|evaluation, dimensions: [...], persona: 'reviewer', backend_used: bool, latency_ms} within 2 seconds for the rule-based fallback path, and run_plan_gen_eval shall chain Plan → Gen → Eval and return {plan, review, evaluation, status: 'pass'|'needs_revision', backend_used}.

While no AI backend is registered via register_persona_backend, the system shall fall back to deterministic rule-based output (no DB write, no LLM call) and shall preserve REVIEW_DIMENSIONS from reviewer_loop.py untouched; the persona backend hook shall accept a single callable conforming to (phase, payload) -> dict and shall not be invoked outside the 3 phases (plan/generate/evaluate).

If review_kind is not in {'task_review', 'integration'}, target_artifact_ids is empty/non-list, an evaluation score is outside [0.0, 1.0], or backend returns malformed output, the service shall raise ReviewerPersonaError mapped to 4xx {detail:{code:'reviewer_persona.invalid_input'|'reviewer_persona.unauthorized', message}} and shall not regress reviewer_loop's existing API contracts (no rename of REVIEW_DIMENSIONS / no DB mutation).

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall expose backend/services/reviewer_turn_counter.py with public symbols increment, get_count, get_state, reset, should_escalate, list_active, MAX_TURNS_DEFAULT (=3), ReviewerTurnCounterError, plus router backend/routers/reviewer_turn_counter.py mounted at POST /api/reviewer-turn/increment, /reset and GET /api/reviewer-turn/{target_id}, /active. The counter shall be in-memory only (per CLAUDE.md §3 Tier 1 short-term semantics; no DB write) and shall coexist with existing reviewer_loop.py / reviewer_persona.py without modifying them (REUSE invariant).

When increment(target_id) is invoked for the N-th time, the system shall return {target_id, count: N, last_updated_at, escalate: N > threshold (default 3)} within 2 seconds; when should_escalate(target_id, threshold) is invoked, the system shall return True iff the stored count is strictly greater than threshold; list_active() shall return entries sorted by count descending with stable order on ties.

While the counter is operational, the system shall keep state in a process-local dict keyed by target_id (no DB / no Redis), shall expose reset_all_state() for test cleanup, and shall NOT modify the existing reviewer_loop.REVIEW_DIMENSIONS or reviewer_persona.PERSONA_NAME constants (cross-module invariant from T-011-01). The endpoints shall NOT mutate state on GET requests.

If target_id is empty/non-string/over 200 chars, threshold is non-int/<=0/>20, actor_user_id is empty string, or count would overflow MAX_COUNT (1000), the service shall raise ReviewerTurnCounterError mapped to 4xx {detail:{code:'reviewer_turn_counter.invalid_input'|'reviewer_turn_counter.unauthorized'|'reviewer_turn_counter.not_found', message}} and shall NOT mutate state on validation failure.

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/services/swarm/worktree.py shall expose public symbols REPO_ROOT, WORKTREES_BASE (=REPO_ROOT/.worktrees), worktree_path(pool_id, cell_index)->Path, branch_name(pool_id, cell_index)->str, create_worktree, remove_worktree, list_worktrees, is_inside_cell_worktree, InvalidWorktreeArgs; worktree_path shall return WORKTREES_BASE/swarm_{pool_id}/cell_{cell_index} and branch_name shall return 'swarm/{pool_id}/cell-{cell_index}' (deterministic naming for swarm orchestration).

When create_worktree(pool_id, cell_index, workspace_id=None) completes successfully, the module shall emit a 'worktree.created' event to audit_logs via services.memory_service.emit_event with detail containing pool_id / cell_index / path / branch; when remove_worktree completes the module shall emit 'worktree.removed'; audit-emit failure shall NOT abort the worktree operation (silent log, AC-3 invariant).

While the worktree manager is operational, all git operations shall be executed via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec with cwd defaulting to REPO_ROOT (NOT shell=True / no os.system / no subprocess.run blocking call) so the manager is fully async-compatible; the module shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010 main path) and shall NOT redefine SECTION_KEYS / REVIEW_DIMENSIONS / PERSONA_NAME (G15 cross-module invariant).

If pool_id is non-int or <= 0, or cell_index is non-int or < 0, _validate shall raise InvalidWorktreeArgs (a ValueError subclass) which the router shall map to 4xx {detail:{code:'worktree.invalid_input', message}}; create_worktree shall NOT leave a partially-created worktree on validation failure and shall NOT swallow git errors silently — git non-zero exit shall surface to the caller with stderr included.

tickets.title: worktree path → session マッピング (T-M29-01 REUSE / parse_worktree_path / is_worktree_path / find_session_for_path / WORKTREE_PATH_PATTERN regex)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/services/swarm/path_mapper.py shall expose public symbols WORKTREE_PATH_PATTERN (compiled regex matching .worktrees/swarm_{pool_id:int}/cell_{cell_index:int}), parse_worktree_path(path) -> Optional[dict{pool_id, cell_index, relative, worktree_root}], is_worktree_path(path) -> bool, find_session_for_path(path) -> Optional[dict], InvalidPathError; T-M29-01 backend/services/swarm/worktree.py shall be REUSED via worktree_path() / branch_name() helpers without modification (REUSE invariant) — path_mapper is a one-way dep on worktree.py constants.

When parse_worktree_path is invoked with `<repo>/.worktrees/swarm_42/cell_7/subdir/file.py`, it shall return {pool_id: 42, cell_index: 7, relative: 'subdir/file.py', worktree_root: '<repo>/.worktrees/swarm_42/cell_7'}; when the path does NOT match WORKTREE_PATH_PATTERN, parse_worktree_path shall return None (no exception); when find_session_for_path is invoked and no session row references the worktree root, it shall return None gracefully (not 404 / not exception).

While the module is operational, the parsing functions shall be pure (no I/O / no DB / deterministic for identical input), the path_mapper shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010), and shall NOT redefine REPO_ROOT / WORKTREES_BASE (re-import from T-M29-01 worktree.py / G15 single-source invariant); find_session_for_path shall use a process-local LRU cache (functools.lru_cache size=256) so repeated lookups are O(1).

If path is empty/non-string/over 4096 chars, parse_worktree_path shall raise InvalidPathError instead of returning None (distinguishes 'no match' from 'invalid input'); if find_session_for_path receives a non-worktree path it shall return None (not raise); pool_id/cell_index parsed from the path shall be subject to T-M29-01 _validate (re-using InvalidWorktreeArgs semantics so pool_id <= 0 / cell_index < 0 are rejected by the underlying worktree module).

tickets.title: merge conflict 検出 + sequential merge ヘルパー (T-M29-01 worktree REUSE / dry-run merge-tree + sequential plan / fail-fast on conflict)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/services/swarm/sequential_merge.py shall expose public symbols MergeConflictError, SequentialMergeError, detect_conflict_dry_run(base_branch, target_branch) -> dict{conflicts:list[str], has_conflict:bool}, plan_sequential_merge(pool_id, target_branch, base='main') -> list[dict], MAX_CELLS_PER_PLAN=64; the helper shall REUSE T-M29-01 worktree.py (worktree_path / branch_name / _run_git) without modifying that file and shall NOT introduce new git binaries (only git already on PATH via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec).

When detect_conflict_dry_run(base, target) is invoked, the helper shall run `git merge-tree base target` via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec (NO `--abort` / NO actual merge / dry-run only), parse the output for `<<<<<<<` markers, and return {has_conflict, conflicts:[file paths], stdout_sample, returncode} within 2 seconds for a typical worktree; when plan_sequential_merge is invoked for pool_id with N cells, the helper shall return a deterministic ordered plan (cell_index ascending) of {pool_id, cell_index, branch, predicted_conflict:bool} entries up to MAX_CELLS_PER_PLAN=64.

While the helper is operational, git operations shall use asyncio.create_subprocess_exec (NO shell=True / NO os.system / NO blocking subprocess.run on git commands), cwd shall default to REPO_ROOT, the module shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm (ADR-010), and shall NOT redefine REPO_ROOT / WORKTREES_BASE (REUSE worktree.py / G15 single-source); detect_conflict_dry_run shall NOT mutate the working tree (no `git merge` / no `git checkout` — `merge-tree` only).

If pool_id <= 0 / cell_index < 0 / base or target branch is empty/non-string, the helper shall raise SequentialMergeError (ValueError subclass) before invoking git so callers map to 4xx; if `git merge-tree` exits non-zero (e.g. unknown ref), the helper shall raise MergeConflictError with stderr surfaced (no silent skip); plan_sequential_merge shall reject N > MAX_CELLS_PER_PLAN with SequentialMergeError; the helper shall NOT actually merge into main / shall NOT push to remote (dry-run / planning only).

tickets.title: resume 機能 (4 択: from_checkpoint/再実行/cancel/手動修正 / round-trip: UI button → POST → handle_resume → status 遷移 → audit → fetch refresh / useSessionResume hook)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: frontend/src/hooks/useSessionResume.ts shall expose a useSessionResume(sessionId, opts) hook returning {resume, isResuming, lastChoice, lastStatus, error}; the hook shall call resumeAgentSession (T-010d-03 helper) wrapping POST /api/agent/sessions/{id}/resume (T-S0-08 backend), then re-fetch via fetchAgentSession to update session state (round-trip completion); VALID_RESUME_CHOICES shall be re-imported from sessions.ts (NOT redefined / T-010d-03 / T-S0-08 cross-module invariant); backend claude_agent_runner.handle_resume + agent_runner.resume_session shall be REUSED without modification.

When resume(choice) is invoked with choice='cancel' the backend shall set SessionRecord.status='cancelled' via store.finalize_session; choice='manual_fix' shall set status='paused'; choice='from_checkpoint' shall invoke run_task(sdk_session_id=prev.sdk_session_id) preserving SDK session continuity; choice='rerun_full' shall invoke run_task(sdk_session_id=None) starting a fresh SDK session; after each successful round-trip backend/routers/agent_runner.resume_session shall emit audit event action='agent.session.resumed' with detail={session_id, choice, new_status}.

While the hook is mounted, isResuming shall be true only during the POST + re-fetch window (no flicker); the hook shall NOT call backend endpoints from React render phase (only inside the resume callback); lastChoice shall remain the last successful choice across re-renders; the file shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm / reactflow legacy (ADR-010 main path); the deterministic status mapping ('cancel'→'cancelled', 'manual_fix'→'paused', 'from_checkpoint'/'rerun_full'→'running') shall align with claude_agent_runner.handle_resume implementation.

If sessionId is non-finite or <= 0 the hook resume() callback shall set error='invalid sessionId' WITHOUT calling backend (no wasted POST); if choice is not in VALID_RESUME_CHOICES resumeAgentSession shall throw AgentSessionError('agent.invalid_resume_choice', 400) without backend hit; if backend returns 404 the hook shall set error and NOT mutate lastStatus; concurrent resume() calls shall be serialized (second call before first completes shall NOT issue a duplicate POST while isResuming=true).

acceptance_criteria.text: frontend/src/components/swarm/SwarmGrid.tsx shall export a default + named React component SwarmGrid(props: {cells: SwarmCell[], size?: SwarmGridSize, onCellClick?: (cell)=>void, className?: string}) where SwarmGridSize is the literal union '4' | '9' | '16' | '64' (default '16'); the component shall render a CSS Grid with cols = sqrt(size) and shall be reachable via frontend/src/app/dashboard/swarm/page.tsx; SwarmCell type shall include {session_id, status, pool_id, cell_index, label?} and the file shall REUSE T-010d-03 SwarmSessionStatus / sessions.ts types (no type duplication).

When the user clicks a cell, onCellClick(cell) shall be invoked within 100ms passing the SwarmCell payload; when size > 16 (i.e. 64), the component shall use windowing/virtualization to render at most 16 cells per viewport step (React.useMemo + visible slice / containerized scroll) so 64-cell grids stay smooth (<= 16ms render budget per frame).

While the grid is rendered, each cell shall apply the 4-status palette identical to SwarmSessionDetail (running=border-eb-500 / done=border-eb-700 / crashed=border-rose-500 / paused=border-amber-500) maintaining cross-component visual consistency (CLAUDE.md §5.2 ENGINE BASE green); the component shall NOT use emoji (Lucide-only / CLAUDE.md §5.1) and shall NOT redefine SwarmSessionStatus enum (import from sessions.ts / G15 single-source).

If cells.length > size, the component shall slice to the first `size` cells and emit no error (graceful overflow); if size is not in (4, 9, 16, 64), the component shall fall back to default 16 (no crash); the component shall NOT call backend endpoints directly (layer separation; page handles data, component renders) and shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm / reactflow legacy (ADR-010 main path).

tickets.title: swarm_session_detail UI (個別全画面 + ライブログ / GET /api/agent/sessions/{id} REUSE / log line timestamp + tool + status palette + Lucide check / 4-status カラー)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: frontend/src/components/sessions/SwarmSessionDetail.tsx shall export a default + named React component SwarmSessionDetail(props: {session, logs, onResume?, onCancel?, className?}) consuming SwarmSessionData / SwarmLogLine types from frontend/src/lib/api/sessions.ts (REUSE existing fetch helper); the component shall be reachable via frontend/src/app/sessions/[id]/page.tsx (Next.js App Router dynamic segment) and shall import the GET /api/agent/sessions/{id} endpoint contract from sessions.ts (no fetch duplication).

When the page mounts with a session_id route param, it shall call fetchAgentSession(sessionId) (typed wrapper around GET /api/agent/sessions/{id}) and render the SwarmSessionDetail component with the response; when onResume(choice) is invoked the page shall call POST /api/agent/sessions/{id}/resume with one of VALID_RESUME_CHOICES = ('from_checkpoint', 'rerun_full', 'manual_fix', 'cancel') matching T-S0-08 backend invariant.

While the component renders, the system shall apply a 4-status palette for session.status (running / done / crashed / paused) using border-eb-500 for running, border-eb-700 for done, border-rose-500 for crashed, border-amber-500 for paused (CLAUDE.md §5.2 ENGINE BASE green); log lines shall render with 3 column layout (time / tool / status) using Lucide check icon for completed steps; the file shall NOT use emoji characters (Lucide-only per CLAUDE.md §5.1) and shall NOT redefine the design tokens.

If the session fetch returns 404, the page shall render an empty-state with a 'session not found' message (no crash); if logs is empty, the component shall render a 'No logs yet' placeholder (no error); the component shall NOT call backend endpoints directly from the component file (data-fetching is the page's responsibility, layer separation invariant — only sessions.ts exposes fetch helpers); reactflow legacy / langgraph / langchain / litellm imports shall NOT appear (ADR-010 main path).

tickets.title: 自動 reconnect + 履歴 fetch (T-010d-01 WS subscribe REUSE / exponential backoff / since_seq=last+1 resume / REST replay 履歴復元 / useSwarmSessionStream hook)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: frontend/src/lib/api/sessions-ws.ts shall expose a SessionStreamClient class (start / close / on(event, handler)) and frontend/src/hooks/useSwarmSessionStream.ts shall expose a useSwarmSessionStream(sessionId, opts) hook returning {logs, connected, lastSeq, reconnectAttempt, error} using the backend contract: WS /api/ws/sessions/{id}?since_seq=N + GET /api/sessions/{id}/replay?since_seq=N (T-010d-01 REUSE without modifying backend/routers/ws.py); the new files shall NOT redefine VALID_RESUME_CHOICES (T-S0-08 / T-010d-03 cross-module invariant — re-import from sessions.ts only).

When the WebSocket disconnects unexpectedly (close code not in CLEAN_CLOSE_CODES = (1000, 1001, 1008)), SessionStreamClient shall schedule a reconnect with exponential backoff starting at INITIAL_RECONNECT_MS = 1000 and doubling up to MAX_RECONNECT_MS = 30000 (capped); after MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS = 8 the client shall stop retrying and emit a single 'reconnect_exhausted' event. When reconnecting, the URL query shall include since_seq=lastSeq+1 so dropped messages are replayed (deterministic resume contract with T-010d-01).

While the hook is mounted, useSwarmSessionStream shall first invoke fetchSessionReplay (= GET /api/sessions/{id}/replay?since_seq=0) to populate the initial log history (no flash-of-empty), then open the WS for live updates; the hook shall use AbortController for the REST fetch and shall close the WS on unmount; the hook shall NOT call backend endpoints from inside the React render phase (only inside useEffect callbacks); the file shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm / reactflow (ADR-010 main path invariant).

If sessionId is non-finite (NaN / undefined / <= 0), the hook shall NOT open a WebSocket and shall set error='invalid sessionId' without throwing; if replay fetch returns 404, the hook shall keep logs=[] and connected=false (graceful empty state, no crash); if a clean close (code 1000) is received, the client shall NOT reconnect (avoid infinite reconnect after server-initiated graceful shutdown); the SessionStreamClient.close() shall be idempotent (multiple calls do not throw).

tickets.title: Langfuse self-host docker-compose (v3 / langfuse-web + langfuse-worker + langfuse-clickhouse + langfuse-minio / main compose と分離 / optional merge / port 3001)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: docker-compose.langfuse.yml shall declare 4 services: langfuse-web (image langfuse/langfuse:3, port 3001:3000 to avoid conflict with frontend default 3000), langfuse-worker (image langfuse/langfuse-worker:3), langfuse-clickhouse (image clickhouse/clickhouse-server with named volume), langfuse-minio (image minio/minio with named volume); main docker-compose.yml shall be left UNCHANGED (separate file for optional merge via `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.langfuse.yml up -d`).

When `docker compose -f docker-compose.langfuse.yml config --quiet` is executed, the parsed config shall list exactly the 4 langfuse-* services with depends_on chain (web depends_on worker + clickhouse + minio; worker depends_on clickhouse); the langfuse-web environment shall include DATABASE_URL pointing to the existing supabase postgres service (postgres:5432) and CLICKHOUSE_URL pointing to langfuse-clickhouse:8123 so the existing Build-Factory database is reused (T-S0-01 REUSE) and no second postgres instance is launched.

While langfuse-web is running, the system shall expose the dashboard at http://localhost:3001 and shall persist data via 2 named volumes: langfuse_clickhouse_data + langfuse_minio_data; .env.langfuse.example shall document the minimum 8 required ENV (NEXTAUTH_SECRET / SALT / ENCRYPTION_KEY / TELEMETRY_ENABLED / LANGFUSE_INIT_ORG_NAME / LANGFUSE_INIT_USER_EMAIL / LANGFUSE_INIT_PROJECT_PUBLIC_KEY / LANGFUSE_INIT_PROJECT_SECRET_KEY) so first-time bootstrap is deterministic; the file shall NOT contain real secrets (placeholders only).

If docker-compose.langfuse.yml mutates / overrides any service from docker-compose.yml (postgres / redis / backend / litellm / frontend), the spec test shall fail; if any langfuse-* service uses image tag 'latest' instead of pinned major version (langfuse:3 / clickhouse-server:24 / minio:RELEASE-2024-* with concrete tag), the test shall fail (reproducibility invariant); the file shall NOT contain any sk-ant-* / supabase service_key / real LANGFUSE_*_KEY values (only placeholders like 'change-me' / hex placeholder).

tickets.title: cost dashboard (8 タブ・Recharts / GET /api/observability/cost-summary?dimension=DIM / cost_logs REUSE / eb-500 palette / Lucide tabs)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: backend/routers/cost_dashboard.py shall expose GET /api/observability/cost-summary?dimension=DIM&from=ISO&to=ISO returning {dimension, from, to, total_usd, total_input_tokens, total_output_tokens, total_cache_read_tokens, items:[{label,cost_usd,input_tokens,output_tokens,share}]} where DIM is one of 8 dimensions (VALID_DIMENSIONS = ('overview','provider','model','workspace','persona','skill','period_daily','session')); frontend/src/app/dashboard/costs/page.tsx shall render a 8-tab dashboard via @/components/dashboard/CostDashboard.tsx using Recharts (BarChart / LineChart / PieChart) with the ENGINE BASE eb-500 palette (CLAUDE.md §5.2); the existing backend/services/cost_service.py shall be REUSED without modification.

When the user clicks one of 8 tabs (Overview / Provider / Model / Workspace / Persona / Skill / Period / Session), the page shall call fetchCostSummary(dimension, range) which issues GET /api/observability/cost-summary; when from/to date inputs change, the request shall be re-issued with new query params; the backend SQL shall use a single SELECT against cost_logs GROUP BY the dimension column (no N+1 query) and shall complete within 2 seconds for ≤ 10k rows.

While the dashboard is mounted, the URL search params shall reflect the active tab (?dim=DIM) and date range (?from=ISO&to=ISO) so deep links work; Recharts components shall apply eb-500 / eb-700 / amber-500 / rose-500 colors only (no hard-coded hex outside Tailwind palette); the component shall NOT call backend endpoints from inside the React render phase (only inside event handlers and useEffect); the file shall NOT import langgraph / langchain / litellm / reactflow (ADR-010 main path).

If dimension is not in VALID_DIMENSIONS the backend shall return 400 {detail:{code:'cost_dashboard.invalid_dimension', message}} without scanning cost_logs; if from/to is not ISO-8601 the backend shall return 400 {detail:{code:'cost_dashboard.invalid_date_range', message}}; if cost_logs has no rows in the requested range the response shall return total_usd=0 with items=[] (graceful empty); the dashboard component shall render an empty-state ('データがありません') instead of crashing; concurrent fetch requests for the same dimension shall be deduplicated via AbortController.

tickets.title: nightly-backup workflow (DB + Storage + 検証 / GitHub Actions cron 17:00 UTC = JST 02:00 / pg_dump + storage list / sha256 verify / 7-day retention)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: .github/workflows/nightly-backup.yml shall declare on.schedule.cron='0 17 * * *' (= UTC 17:00 / JST 02:00 nightly), on.workflow_dispatch (manual trigger with reason input), and 3 jobs: backup-database (pg_dump → gzip → sha256), backup-storage (supabase storage list → tarball → sha256), verify-backup (re-compute sha256 + assert size > 0); concurrency.group='nightly-backup' with cancel-in-progress=false (concurrent run 防止 + 履歴保持) and permissions defaulting to read-only (contents: read) — write は不要.

When the nightly cron fires (or workflow_dispatch is invoked), the workflow shall run backup-database then backup-storage in parallel (needs: []) and then verify-backup (needs: [backup-database, backup-storage]); each backup step shall produce an artifact named with date suffix (db-{YYYYMMDD}.sql.gz / storage-{YYYYMMDD}.tar.gz) uploaded via actions/upload-artifact@v4 with retention-days=7; verify-backup shall download both artifacts and assert sha256 hashes match the pre-computed values stored in step output (no silent corruption).

While the workflow is operational, secrets shall be referenced as ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_DB_URL }} / ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }} / ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_STORAGE_URL }} (never hard-coded); the workflow shall NOT touch production data (read-only pg_dump + read-only storage list); the workflow file shall be pure YAML with no shell injection vulnerability (all user inputs / secrets shall be passed via env: section, NOT inlined into `run:` strings via string interpolation).

If pg_dump exits non-zero or the resulting gzip file is empty (< 100 bytes), backup-database shall fail the workflow with explicit error message; if the sha256 verification in verify-backup mismatches, the workflow shall fail and (in a follow-up Sprint) post a Slack alert; the workflow shall NOT use actions versions newer than ubuntu-latest official images, and shall pin every action to a specific version (actions/checkout@v4 / actions/upload-artifact@v4 / actions/download-artifact@v4) — no @main / @master / floating refs.

tickets.title: WorkspaceService.bootstrap() — 新案件作成時にテンプレを GitHub repo に展開
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: When a new workspace is created via POST /api/workspaces, the system shall automatically create a GitHub repo (engine-base/proj-{slug}) and populate it with templates/project-bootstrap/ files.
When the bootstrap completes, the system shall make an initial commit with message 'chore: bootstrap project from template v{X}' on the main branch.
While the bootstrap is in progress, the system shall set workspace.status='bootstrapping' and refuse other operations until status becomes 'ready'.
Where the user provided a custom GitHub org, the system shall create the repo under that org instead of the default.
If the GitHub API call fails, the system shall mark workspace.status='bootstrap_failed', preserve the workspace row for retry, and shall not leave a partial repo on GitHub.

tickets.title: Jinja2 プレースホルダ置換エンジン
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall render every .j2 file in templates/project-bootstrap/ using Jinja2 with the workspace metadata as context.
The system shall support 10 placeholders: project_name, project_slug, client_name, deadline, phase, owner_email, tech_stack, ai_employees, template_version, generated_at.
When a required placeholder is missing from the workspace metadata, the system shall raise BootstrapError before writing any file (atomic).
While rendering, the system shall apply autoescape=False (Markdown / HTML output preserved).
If a rendered file still contains {{ }} (unrendered placeholder), the system shall fail validation and shall not commit.

tickets.title: 既存案件への遡及適用 (build-factory project migrate)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The CLI command `build-factory project migrate --workspace={id}` shall fetch the workspace's repo, diff with the latest template, and add only missing files.
When an existing file would be overwritten, the system shall skip it and report a manual-merge needed.
While dry-run mode is on (--dry-run), the system shall print the diff but shall not commit or push.
Where --all is specified, the system shall iterate through every workspace and migrate sequentially.
If the workspace repo is dirty (uncommitted changes), the system shall abort the migration and shall not force any change.

tickets.title: テンプレ更新時に全案件へ PR 自動作成 (CI 統合)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When templates/CHANGELOG.md is updated on main, the GitHub Action shall trigger a dry-run migrate against every active workspace.
The system shall report the total number of files that would change per workspace, awaiting masato approval before proceeding.
When masato approves via the workflow_dispatch input, the system shall create a PR titled 'chore: migrate to template v{X}' on each workspace repo.
While a workspace's last migration is pending review, the system shall not create a duplicate PR for the same template version.
If any workspace migration fails (auth / network), the system shall continue with the others and report failures in the summary, not abort the whole job.

tickets.title: e2e テスト = workspace 作成 → 強制レイヤー検証
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When the e2e test creates a new workspace, the resulting GitHub repo shall contain CLAUDE.md, docs/HANDOVER.md, scripts/lint-mock.sh, scripts/validate-tickets.py, and .claude/settings.json.
The test shall verify that grep -c 'Build-Factory' CLAUDE.md returns >= 1 in the bootstrapped repo.
When the test runs `bash scripts/lint-mock.sh` on the bootstrapped repo, it shall pass (no emojis, no AGPL, no missing files).
While running CI, this test shall execute under 60 seconds.
If any required file is missing from the bootstrapped repo, the test shall fail with a clear error indicating the missing path.

tickets.title: LiteLLM Router (サブ用途のみ — 画像/音声/安価バッチ/緊急代替)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: LiteLLM shall be used ONLY for sub-purpose routes (image generation, speech, batch with cheaper providers, emergency Anthropic-outage fallback) — NOT for the main Claude Code implementation engine.
When an image-gen request arrives, the system shall route to Gemini Image / DALL-E via LiteLLM.
When Anthropic API is unhealthy (3 consecutive 5xx within 60s), the system shall switch the main route to LiteLLM → GPT-4o or Gemini 2.5 Pro for the duration of the outage.
While in fallback mode, the system shall mark every response with provider=fallback and disable Memory API writes (which only Anthropic supports).
If a developer attempts to route the main Claude Code engine through LiteLLM (e.g., importing LiteLLM from claude-runner code), the lint script shall fail.

tickets.title: Anthropic Memory API 統合 (永続記憶の主たる保存先)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall integrate Anthropic Memory API as the primary store for cross-session durable facts (decisions, preferences, context).
When a session ends with status=completed, the system shall extract durable facts (D-XXX style) and write them to Memory API scoped by user/workspace.
When a new session starts, the system shall recall relevant Memory API entries and inject them into the system prompt within 200ms.
While storing facts, the system shall tag each entry with source_session_id and confidence_score (0.0–1.0).
Where the user requests memory deletion, the system shall remove the fact from Memory API + Mem0 + Obsidian within 24 hours and emit an audit event.
If Memory API write fails, the system shall queue the fact for retry and shall not lose the data.

tickets.title: Mem0 ベクトル検索 + Anthropic Memory API ブリッジ
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The bridge shall mirror every Memory API write to Mem0 (pgvector) for similarity search.
When a session asks 'recall similar past decisions to X', the system shall query Mem0 vector top-5 within 300ms and re-rank against Memory API results.
While the user is the secretary AI, the bridge shall preload top-50 most-relevant facts into the system prompt at session start.
If Mem0 and Memory API diverge (e.g., a fact exists in one but not the other), the bridge shall surface the discrepancy in audit_logs and shall not silently pick one.

tickets.title: chat_messages 全文検索 (pg_trgm + pgvector ハイブリッド)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: The system shall expose a hybrid search API combining pg_trgm (lexical similarity) and pgvector (semantic similarity) over chat_messages.content.
When a user types a query in S-011 global search, the system shall return ranked results (hybrid score = 0.5*trgm + 0.5*vector) within 500ms (P95).
Where the user adds 'date:2026-04', the system shall narrow results to that date range.
While indexing, the system shall use pg_partman to partition chat_messages by month for fast archival.
If a query attempts to search across workspaces the user has no access to, RLS shall block it and the result count shall be 0.

tickets.title: Constitution 自動注入エンジン (全 AI 社員のシステムプロンプト)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: Every AI employee session shall have the latest Constitution version (masato's judgment criteria) injected into the system prompt as a stable cached prefix.
When the Constitution is updated (new version saved in S-018), the system shall invalidate prompt cache for all active sessions on next turn.
While a session is operating as 'secretary' AI, the system shall additionally inject the full constitution body (all sections); for other roles, only Sections 2 (values) + 4 (red lines).
Where a workspace overrides specific constitution sections (workspace-level Constitution), the system shall merge with masato's base constitution, workspace winning on conflict.
If the Constitution file is corrupted or missing, the system shall block all AI sessions and alert masato — it shall not run AI without constitution.

tickets.title: Cost tracking (Anthropic Usage API + LiteLLM callback で 案件/AI/日次集計)
tickets.label: REFACTOR

acceptance_criteria.text: Every Claude API call (via claude-agent-sdk) and every LiteLLM call shall record one cost_logs row containing: provider, model, in/out tokens, cache_read/write tokens, USD cost, session_id, workspace_id, ai_employee_id.
When a session completes, the system shall reconcile its cumulative cost with Anthropic Usage API (daily) and flag discrepancies > 5%.
While monthly cost on a workspace exceeds the configured budget, the system shall pause that workspace's queue and notify masato.
While prompt cache is hit, cost_logs.cache_read_tokens shall be > 0 and the displayed cost shall reflect the 90% discount on cached input.
If cost recording fails, the session shall continue but emit a cost_recording_failed audit event for manual reconciliation.

tickets.title: Rate limit 自動 retry (tenacity 指数バックオフ 2/4/8/16s)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When Anthropic API returns 429 (rate limit) or 529 (overloaded), the system shall retry with exponential backoff: 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, then fail.
When a transient 5xx is returned, the system shall retry up to 3 times before failing.
While retrying, the system shall preserve the session_id and idempotency-key so the same logical request is not duplicated.
If a non-retryable error (4xx other than 429) is returned, the system shall NOT retry and shall surface the error immediately.

tickets.title: Streaming UI (claude-agent-sdk → WebSocket bridge)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When claude-agent-sdk emits a streaming token, the system shall forward it via WebSocket to the subscribed frontend within 50ms.
When a tool_use event occurs, the system shall send a structured ws message {type:'tool_use', tool, input} and append to session_logs.
While the WebSocket disconnects, the system shall buffer messages for 30s and replay on reconnect.
Where the client requests log replay, the system shall replay session_logs since a given seq_id.
If the buffer overflows (> 1MB per session), the system shall drop the oldest streaming messages and emit a buffer_overflow event but shall preserve tool_use and final result.

tickets.title: Anthropic 障害時 LiteLLM フォールバック (Claude → GPT-4o / Gemini)
tickets.label: NEW

acceptance_criteria.text: When Anthropic API health-check fails 3 times within 60 seconds, the system shall switch the main route to LiteLLM → GPT-4o (primary fallback) and notify masato.
While in fallback mode, the system shall disable Memory API writes and Subagent (Task tool), and shall mark every session with degraded_mode=true.
When Anthropic recovers (3 consecutive successful health-checks in 5 min), the system shall switch back to claude-agent-sdk automatically.
Where masato manually overrides via /admin/fallback {provider:gemini}, the system shall route to Gemini 2.5 Pro instead of GPT-4o.

If both Anthropic AND OpenAI fail simultaneously, the system shall pause new sessions, allow only emergency read-only access, and alert masato + Slack — it shall not silently route to an untested provider.
